uk-eu.summary: Of the 10 biggest movers in the uk-eu universe today, the bot held 0 and missed 10. Five upside misses were earnings/results-day catalysts (CURY +15%, IGG +11%, CCR +5%, III +6%, SAP +6% AI launch) where the strategies' earnings gates filtered the names out pre-event and they never re-entered intraday. AAF +7% was a parent-company M&A move that the morning news brief didn't flag as a UK-listed catalyst. The four downside misses (PRN, CWR, RPI, HOC) are unreachable by the long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short. CWR is the most instructive: a +929% YoY momentum darling finally rolling over after a Weichai-fuelled parabolic week, the exact kind of profit-taking signal a future short-side or paired-trade strategy could harvest.

top_movers.catalyst: FY results: H1 sales growth accelerating, dividend resumed after being passed last year; net cash above £100m target.
top_movers.miss_reason: Results day catalyst not flagged in pre-market news brief; mean-reverter excluded (stock not at oversold extreme), momentum picks chose names with stronger trailing 20d.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-earnings re-entry rule: when an earnings-gated name opens ≥+5% on results day, allow news-reactive to take a half-size position with a tight 1.5% stop.

top_movers.catalyst: AGM + Q1 revenue +19% to £331m, AUM >£20bn, monthly active customers +119% trade growth, forward guidance raised.
top_movers.miss_reason: Stock was at all-time high pre-news (set 2026-05-07); mean-reverter excluded for high RSI, momentum-trader didn't flag because the 5d trailing return was modest going in.
top_movers.suggestion: Momentum-trader treats new ATHs as 'extended' and skips them; allow entry when the ATH coincides with a fundamental beat (revenue ≥+15% YoY).

top_movers.catalyst: Bharti Airtel parent raising stake to ~79% via $2.9bn share swap (cleared 2026-05-13); FY revenue $6.4bn (+30% YoY).
top_movers.miss_reason: EM telecom is a category-edge UK-listed name; news-reactive's morning brief didn't pick up the parent-company M&A as a London-listed catalyst.
top_movers.suggestion: Extend news-reactive's morning brief to include parent-company M&A filings for UK-listed subsidiaries (Bharti↔AAF, Vodafone↔VOD, etc).

top_movers.catalyst: Sapphire 2026 conference: 'Autonomous Enterprise' AI strategy + multi-vendor partnerships (Microsoft, Anthropic, AWS, NVIDIA, Palantir); Deutsche Bank reiterated Buy at €200.
top_movers.miss_reason: Mega-cap AI continuation; strategies were rotating into mid-cap names today, and macro-aligned's regime signals didn't carry an explicit AI-theme tilt.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a one-day AI-thematic tilt to macro-aligned when a vendor announces a major-platform launch (Sapphire, AWS re:Invent, Google Cloud Next).

top_movers.catalyst: FY26 results: total return on funds 22%, NAV/share +22% to 3,030p, £750m buyback, dividend raised. Buy-the-news bounce after pre-results selloff on Action LFL concerns.
top_movers.miss_reason: Falls between strategy windows: mean-reverter saw a rebound already in motion (not a clean oversold setup), momentum-trader saw it still mid-pullback going in. Earnings filter excluded it as a pre-event candidate.
top_movers.suggestion: On results day, mean-reverter should evaluate against the pre-results SMA20 (not today's), since a 'rebound from oversold' setup gets erased once the news fires.

top_movers.catalyst: FY26 earnings day: branded drinks +4% rev / €51m OP (Tennent's, Bulmers) overshadowed weaker MCB distribution; division restructuring announced.
top_movers.miss_reason: Earnings-day pick; bot's earnings gate filters pre-results candidates, and post-catalyst entry has no setup signal once the move is already in the price.
top_movers.suggestion: Earnings gate currently filters all results-day candidates; allow re-entry within 60min of open if segment commentary contradicts the headline miss.

top_movers.catalyst: (no clear LSE-listed PRN catalyst surfaced in web search; obscure small-cap, possibly delisted or thinly covered)
top_movers.miss_reason: Long-only basket — no short capability. Universe contains the ticker but strategies have no mechanism to express a bearish view.
top_movers.suggestion: (no actionable change — long-only basket; would need a short-side or paired-trade capability to express)

top_movers.catalyst: Profit-taking after a hyper-extended run: +929% YoY / +32% past week on the Weichai licensing deal + Rothschild target hike (£4.60 from £2.05) on solid-oxide-fuel-cell AI-DC theme.
top_movers.miss_reason: Long-only — strategies can't short. Mean-reverter would have excluded it for being far above SMA20; momentum-trader wouldn't enter a name showing its first major down day after a parabolic run.
top_movers.suggestion: Worth designing a 'parabolic reversal' short signal: 5d return >20%, RSI>75, first close below SMA5 — exactly the CWR pattern. Requires short capability.

top_movers.catalyst: Lowest share price since IPO; continuation of H1 revenue -6% disappointment and recent CFO departure announcement context.
top_movers.miss_reason: Long-only — no short capability. Mean-reverter screens for a setup not a falling knife; news-reactive saw negative drift without a positive contra-signal.
top_movers.suggestion: (no actionable change — continuation on prior weakness, no contra-signal; would benefit from a short-side strategy or pair-trade against a rallying peer)

top_movers.catalyst: Precious-metals miner pullback after a strong Q1 production beat run (gold realised price $4,471/oz drove balance sheet swing to ~$95m net cash); commodity profit-taking.
top_movers.miss_reason: Long-only — no short capability. Commodity-momentum's tilt is oil/USO not precious-metals miners; other strategies don't separately model miner sub-sector pullbacks.
top_movers.suggestion: Add precious-metals miners as a separate commodity-momentum sub-universe so the strategy can rotate between oil, gold, and miners on relative-strength signals.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Trades bond ETFs based on yield-curve shape, central-bank
stance, and credit spreads. US: TLT/IEF/SHY/HYG/LQD. UK/EU: Gilts +
Bunds + EUR credit ETFs. Same strategy bias, different bond universe
per region.

uncommitted.rationale: 30Y at 5.18% with no Fed cut catalyst, TLT outflow news, and sustained macro bearishness override the deeply oversold RSI-29 reading — any bounce is a fade.
Duration + investment-grade credit = double headwind; 30Y at 5.15% is a structural cap-rate ceiling and IG has been the worst performer in the HY-vs-IG spread dynamic.
RSI 29.1 is oversold but both SMAs are falling overhead and the rate regime offers no yield-compression catalyst to drive a sustained reversal.
Benign credit spread picture (HY outperforming IG 5d) is the one supportive signal, but price is below both SMAs and stagflationary macro is a slow headwind.
Low duration insulates from long-rate pressure but Fed-on-hold means no short-end rally; ATR of $0.10 virtually guarantees the return can't clear the 0.60% cost gate regardless of direction.
10Y above 4.6% and 30Y at 5.13% with term premium still building; TLT outflows accelerating and no macro catalyst for long-end relief today.
Medium-duration Treasury faces same rate headwinds as TLT but less severity; below all MAs with negative MACD and no catalyst for reversal.
Duration-led IG corporate selloff continues as long-end rates hold elevated; spread cushion insufficient to offset rate drag at 30Y 5.13%.
HY-IG differential +0.53pp (risk-on credit) provides partial offset to rate pressure; shortest effective duration of the credit names keeps drawdown contained.
Minimal duration insulates from long-end selloff; Fed on hold anchors front-end rates with no near-term policy catalyst in either direction.

active.description:

stance, and credit spreads. US: TLT/IEF/SHY/HYG/LQD. UK/EU: Gilts +

executed.thesis: Short-duration gilts are the only technically clean name in this universe — above both MAs with positive 5d momentum. In a stagflationary 'no cuts' regime, short duration minimises rate-path risk while maintaining bond exposure; yield anchoring at the short end keeps carry stable.
executed.outcome_notes: Price returned exactly to entry by the scheduled exit — no directional move materialized in either direction, leaving transaction friction as the sole P&L driver. The technical setup held (no breakdown), but the 'no cuts' regime produced stasis rather than the expected yield-anchoring bid on the short end.
executed.risks_observed: A technically clean instrument in a low-volatility session becomes a pure friction trade; the setup offered no margin to recover spread costs without intraday momentum. Scheduled exit removed any ability to hold for a late-session catalyst, making the hold period the binding constraint rather than the thesis.

executed.thesis: HY credit is the one constructive macro signal: HY outperformed IG by 62bp last week and IHYG holds above SMA50, confirming spreads are not widening. This is a credit-resilience trade, not a duration trade — sized smaller given thin volume and tighter take-profit to reflect the limited upside in a stagflationary backdrop.
executed.outcome_notes: No price movement from entry to exit — the prior week's HY outperformance signal had already been absorbed, and no incremental spread compression occurred within the holding window. The thin-volume flag proved relevant: the effective spread likely accounts for the bulk of the loss on an otherwise flat print.
executed.risks_observed: Both basket legs closed negative on flat prices, pointing to a session where transaction costs dominate and no strategy recovers friction without directional follow-through. HY in a stagflationary backdrop also carries asymmetric gap risk on any sudden risk-off print — a smaller position helps but doesn't eliminate that tail, and there was no stop to contain it intraday.

executed.thesis: The 5d HY-IG outperformance (+38bp) confirms this is a pure duration selloff, not a credit-stress event — HY spreads are stable to tightening, which directly benefits IHYG.L. It is the sole candidate above both SMA20 and SMA50 with positive MACD and RSI 52, making it the only technically-valid bond expression in the current rising-rate, no-recession regime.
executed.outcome_notes: Price was flat entry-to-exit at $0.91, producing a near-zero loss attributable to friction rather than adverse price movement. The scheduled exit fired before any meaningful directional move developed, so the thesis — that stable HY spreads in a duration-led selloff would lift IHYG.L — was neither confirmed nor refuted; the hold period was simply too short to observe the expected spread-compression tailwind.
executed.risks_observed: Single-name concentration with no paired IG short left the position fully exposed to any abrupt credit-risk repricing that would have invalidated the HY-IG divergence premise mid-hold. The scheduled exit mechanism, while clean, removed the possibility of riding a delayed thesis realisation and also means there is no price-action evidence to validate or challenge the technical setup for future entries.

executed.thesis: Credit spread dynamics favour HY over IG — HYG 5d -0.85% vs LQD -1.23% (HY-IG diff +0.38pp, risk-on) with no credit-stress signal; IHYG.L holds above both SMA20 and SMA50, RSI 53 non-extended, leaving room to run. Duration exposure avoided entirely: 10Y broke above 4.6% and 30Y at 5.13% makes TLT/IEF toxic in a stagflationary rising-rate regime — credit-only is the only viable bond-cycle expression today.
executed.outcome_notes: The position exited flat at the entry price on a scheduled close — no price movement was captured in either direction. The HY-over-IG relative value thesis never had a chance to express itself intraday; credit spreads either didn't move enough or IHYG.L's illiquidity suppressed any price discovery that occurred in the underlying.
executed.risks_observed: The zero P&L outcome highlights execution risk in a thinly traded LSE-listed ETF: scheduled exits at mid-price in low-volume instruments frequently round-trip to zero net of spread, making the strategy reliant on multi-day holds to realise any edge. The single-name concentration in one HY vehicle with no IG or short-duration hedge means any sudden credit-stress episode (spread blowout, risk-off spike) would hit the full position with no offset.

uncommitted.rationale: Longest duration in the universe with RSI 35.9 in a confirmed downtrend — rising real rates provide no technical or fundamental floor.
uncommitted.reflection: RSI 35.9 downtrend signal was directionally correct but overstated the move — actual drop was deeper than predicted, not a flat.

uncommitted.rationale: Best 5d return of the duration names and healthy volume, but still below both MAs — BoE/Fed rate-path divergence is possible but not yet priced or confirmed.
uncommitted.reflection: Best-5d-return optimism was neutralised by persistent below-MA positioning; rate-path divergence never catalysed the predicted flat-positive.

uncommitted.rationale: Euro government bonds face the same real-rate headwind as UK/US duration; MACD histogram divergence (-0.063) is the most bearish of the group.
uncommitted.reflection: MACD histogram bearish signal was correct in direction; real-rate headwind kept the move within flat territory rather than extending to mild_down.

uncommitted.rationale: Global aggregate bond in persistent 20d decline; mixed-duration exposure doesn't insulate from the macro rate headwind, and MACD remains negative.
uncommitted.reflection: Macro rate headwind assumed persistent decline, but mixed-duration cushioning absorbed the pressure — actual move was near-zero vs predicted mild_down.

uncommitted.rationale: IG corporate above SMA50 but rate-sensitivity dominates; MACD flat and volume negligible — essentially range-bound with no directional catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Above-SMA50 stability held; rate sensitivity was flagged but didn't materialise enough to push beyond the flat band predicted.

uncommitted.rationale: High-duration global govt basket is the direct casualty of the oil-driven long-end steepening; RSI 36.6 in clear downtrend below both MAs.
UK gilts under pressure from global steepening impulse; below both MAs, RSI 42.7, no BoE cut catalyst on the horizon.
Euro govts have moderate duration drag from steepening; below both MAs with negative MACD, though slightly better relative to UK/global equivalents given ECB path divergence.
IG credit faces dual headwinds (duration + spread), but spreads not blowing out; extremely low volume (0.12x) and below MAs suggest continued quiet drift lower.
Medium-duration gilts most stable of the govt names but stale data (May 15), ultra-low volume (0.07x), and below MAs limit conviction in either direction.
Short duration insulates most from long-end steepening, but still below both MAs with negative MACD; no positive catalyst to hold.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Trades commodity ETFs based on dollar index, real yields,
supply/demand themes. Commodities are dollar-denominated globally so the
same US-listed ETF universe (GLD, SLV, USO, DBA, DBB) is used in both
regions — only the underlying decision logic differs by macro regime.

executed.thesis: Oil supply shock thesis intact and intensifying — Iran military activity and G7 sanctions push add fresh geopolitical premium on top of a technically strong trend (RSI 63.7, above SMA20 by 7%, +17.89% 20d). Expected ~+2.1% day return clears the 0.30% round-trip cost gate by a wide margin; the only commodity with macro, technical, and news alignment today.

Iran nuclear talks explicitly stalling — White House rejected today's proposal as insufficient, keeping Hormuz disruption risk elevated with no near-term resolution pathway. Oil up 24% in 20 days on confirmed supply shock; RSI 63, positive MACD, and price 7.7% above SMA20 confirm the uptrend is intact and unextended.
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted lower from entry to a scheduled exit, with the geopolitical catalyst failing to sustain intraday momentum — likely a case of 'buy the rumor, sell the news' dynamics as the market had already priced the Iran stall. The exit was time-driven rather than signal-driven, so the position was closed regardless of whether the thesis remained live.
executed.risks_observed: A 24% run-up over 20 days meant significant speculative premium was already embedded in the price, making the entry vulnerable to any softening in sentiment even without a fundamental reversal. Scheduled exits remove discretion at precisely the moment when holding or cutting early might be warranted — if the geopolitical driver is still active, a time-based rule captures none of that upside; if it's fading, it holds too long.

uncommitted.rationale: Silver in a structural breakdown — below both SMAs, MACD negative, -14% over 5 days; rising real rates and dollar removes both the monetary and industrial bids simultaneously.
uncommitted.reflection: Real-rate/dollar thesis was directionally right but silver held support — no catalyst pushed it past flat into a confirmed decline.

uncommitted.rationale: Death cross forming with ETF outflows accelerating; real rates rising and dollar firming are dual headwinds, though RSI 37.7 limits downside momentum today.
uncommitted.reflection: Three bearish setups aligned but failed to trigger follow-through; gold absorbed selling pressure and closed flat.

uncommitted.rationale: 5d -3% with MACD histogram turning negative; rising dollar is a direct headwind for USD-priced base metals, and no China demand catalyst to offset.
uncommitted.reflection: Dollar and sector headwinds delivered the predicted direction but magnitude stayed within flat, not reaching the mild_down threshold.

uncommitted.rationale: 20d uptrend technically intact above both SMAs but MACD rolling over with near-zero volume (0.08 ratio) and no identifiable catalyst; expected drift insufficient to clear cost gate.
uncommitted.reflection: No-catalyst flat call correct; MACD deterioration offset the stagflation tailwind for a near-zero session.

uncommitted.rationale: Below both SMAs after an 11.5% 5-day crash; dollar strength and elevated real yields maintain the gold/silver complex selloff
Death cross forming with accelerating ETF outflows; dollar strength and real yields at 4.60% eliminate the monetary bid

active.description:
LLM-driven. Trades commodity ETFs based on dollar index, real yields,
supply/demand themes. Commodities are dollar-denominated globally so the
same US-listed ETF universe (GLD, SLV, USO, DBA, DBB) is used in both
regions — only the underlying decision logic differs by macro regime.

executed.thesis: Oil supply shock thesis is intact — Iran military standoff maintains a risk premium regardless of Monday's tactical delay, and USO's technicals confirm active momentum (above SMA20/50, RSI 62, +23% 20d). Wider stop accommodates intraday diplomatic headline volatility without shaking out on noise.
executed.outcome_notes: USO drifted steadily higher into the scheduled exit, consistent with a market that continued to price geopolitical risk premium without a resolution catalyst. Price action was constructive — no sharp retracements that would have tested the wider stop — suggesting the momentum signal rather than any single headline drove the gain. Exit was time-based, not triggered by thesis invalidation.
executed.risks_observed: A single-name crude proxy concentrates headline risk: any unexpected de-escalation (ceasefire signal, diplomatic statement) could have reversed the premium sharply intraday, and a scheduled exit offers no protection against that gap. With no correlated hedges in the basket, a broad risk-off commodity sell-off would have hit the position without an offset. Hindsight flag: RSI at 62 on entry left limited buffer before overbought territory, compressing the asymmetry of the trade.

executed.thesis: Oil supply shock confirmed by IEA (commercial inventories depleting rapidly with weeks left) and corroborated by price action well above SMA20 ($138.57) and SMA50 ($128.01) with RSI 61.6. Iran deal risk is real but incomplete — US proposal is a temporary OFAC waiver, Iran demands full sanctions removal — leaving supply-tightness thesis intact for today's session.
executed.outcome_notes: Price moved cleanly from entry to exit without meaningful retracement, consistent with sustained supply-tightness rather than a spike-and-fade pattern. The scheduled exit captured a 1.46% move; no adverse intraday reversal forced early closure, suggesting the IEA inventory signal held through the session. Exit was time-driven rather than thesis-invalidating, so the underlying catalyst remained live at close.
executed.risks_observed: Iran deal tail risk was acknowledged at entry but not hedged — a surprise diplomatic breakthrough intraday could have reversed the move sharply, and the scheduled exit provided no downside stop if that had occurred. Single-name commodity ETF concentration with no correlated short (e.g., refiner or demand-side offset) left the position fully exposed to any macro risk-off rotation that tends to hit energy alongside equities. RSI near 62 at entry left room for momentum exhaustion; a stronger reading would have flagged more crowding risk in the name.

uncommitted.rationale: -10.3% in 5 days confirms the industrial-monetary premium is being stripped out as real rates rise and dollar firms; below SMA20 with RSI 49 and no recovery catalyst
uncommitted.reflection: Real-rate/dollar thesis was directionally right but silver held support — no catalyst pushed it past flat into a confirmed decline.

uncommitted.rationale: Death cross approaching, ETF outflows accelerating, and real rates rising — the three conditions for sustained gold weakness are all present simultaneously
uncommitted.reflection: Three bearish setups aligned but failed to trigger follow-through; gold absorbed selling pressure and closed flat.

uncommitted.rationale: Dollar grinding toward 100 is a structural headwind; XLB/Materials is the worst sector in macro view (-3.9% 5d); MACD hist barely positive and 5d momentum already negative
uncommitted.reflection: Dollar and sector headwinds delivered the predicted direction but magnitude stayed within flat, not reaching the mild_down threshold.

uncommitted.rationale: Above SMAs but MACD histogram turning negative and no idiosyncratic catalyst visible; mild macro tailwind from stagflation but no trigger
uncommitted.reflection: No-catalyst flat call correct; MACD deterioration offset the stagflation tailwind for a near-zero session.

uncommitted.rationale: Confirmed downtrend below SMA20 and SMA50, elevated sell-side volume ratio (1.91), and rising real yields remove the monetary-metal bid.
Death cross imminent, ETF outflows accelerating, and the stagflationary regime drives real yield expansion rather than contraction — structurally wrong setup for gold.

active.description:
Deterministic baseline. Buys top-N highest previous-day % gainers from the
configured universe, equal weight, no stops, no take-profits. No LLM
involvement. Region-agnostic — runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and
against UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The yardstick every
other strategy must beat in each region.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.53% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
Top previous-day gainer (+5.02% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.

executed.outcome_notes: BL held its prior-day momentum into the session, advancing a further 1.4% before the scheduled exit. Price action was constructive — no meaningful giveback — suggesting the gap was supported by genuine buying rather than fading enthusiasm.
executed.risks_observed: A single-day momentum filter with no volume or catalyst check means entries like this are indistinguishable from ones that reverse sharply; the win here was directional luck. Three of five names in the basket closed lower, so basket-level correlation to mean-reversion risk was high on the day.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+7.57% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: ABR faded from entry, closing the session below the buy price — the prior-day gap was not sustained, consistent with a low-float name where the opening surge exhausted demand. The scheduled exit captured the full intraday drift lower without any stop intervening.
executed.risks_observed: ABR at ~$6 carries liquidity and spread risk that higher-priced names in the same basket don't; equal-notional sizing effectively overweights it in share terms and amplifies slippage exposure. No intraday stop meant the loss accumulated unimpeded.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+7.50% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: NSP drifted lower through the session, unwinding roughly a point from entry before the scheduled close. The prior-day gap failed to attract follow-through buyers, and price action was soft without a clear catalyst to sustain it.
executed.risks_observed: NSP is a mid-cap HR-services name whose gaps are often driven by earnings or guidance; entering without filtering for whether the move was catalyst-driven introduces the risk of buying exhausted post-event supply. The loss, while modest, was entirely preventable with a basic catalyst check.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.85% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: FDS gave back 1.5% from entry to the scheduled exit, with price declining steadily — the prior session's gain was not confirmed by further buying on this day. No news or sector catalyst was visible to explain the reversal, suggesting the gap simply attracted profit-taking.
executed.risks_observed: At ~$227 per share, FDS carries the highest per-share price in the basket, meaning execution slippage and bid-ask spread are less punishing in percentage terms, but the absolute dollar loss is amplified if the position is sized on notional rather than volatility. The basket ran 3-of-5 losers today, indicating a systematic mean-reversion headwind for the strategy on this date.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.75% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: FICO extended its prior-day move, adding 0.73% to the scheduled exit — the gap held and buyers remained engaged through the session. As the highest-priced name in the basket, even a small percentage gain produced a positive contribution.
executed.risks_observed: FICO's elevated absolute price (~$1,168) means that equal-notional position sizing results in very few shares, concentrating execution risk around the open and close where wide spreads can meaningfully erode a sub-1% gain. The strategy has no mechanism to distinguish this continuation from the reversals seen in ABR, NSP, and FDS on the same day.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.56% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.

executed.risks_observed: Nine duplicate WDAY entries mirror the pattern across all five tickers, strongly suggesting a systemic trade-generation fault rather than intentional sizing. The basket's five-name tech concentration (adtech, SaaS, semiconductors, storage, financial data) means correlation in a risk-off session would be high with no diversification benefit.

active.description:
Deterministic baseline. Buys top-N highest previous-day % gainers from the
configured universe, equal weight, no stops, no take-profits. No LLM
involvement. Region-agnostic — runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and
against UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The yardstick every
other strategy must beat in each region.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+14.25% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted higher through the session and closed near the day's range top, suggesting the large prior-day gap (14.25%) had genuine institutional follow-through rather than noise. The scheduled exit captured that continuation cleanly.
executed.risks_observed: A 14.25% overnight gap in a large-cap name is an extreme outlier and statistically more likely to mean-revert; the win here was not representative of the expected distribution. No stop was in place to protect against the more probable fade scenario.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+11.24% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Momentum from an 11.24% gap carried intraday, and price closed above entry without significant drawdown. The move likely reflected a fundamental catalyst (pharma is prone to news-driven gaps) that retained buying interest through the session.
executed.risks_observed: Pharma gap-ups following news events often see sharp reversals once the initial reaction is digested; holding through a scheduled exit with no stop exposed the position to that tail. Correlation with LSEG — both large-cap gainers on the same day — meant two meaningful positions were exposed to the same macro sentiment shift.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+7.75% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price faded steadily from entry and closed below it, the prior-day gap giving way to profit-taking. The +7.75% prior move was the weakest in the basket and in a small-cap miner, where gaps are more likely driven by thin liquidity than sustained demand.
executed.risks_observed: Small-cap mining names carry outsized spread and liquidity risk; the effective cost of entry and exit erodes a position at this price level faster than the nominal P&L shows. No intraday stop meant the full -3.12% drawdown was absorbed without any decision gate.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+5.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: The gap reversed almost immediately and the position was underwater for most of the session, closing at the low end of the day's range. At a sub-£3.10 price point, the prior +5.38% gain was likely thin-book noise rather than a signal of directional momentum.
executed.risks_observed: This is the largest percentage loss in the basket and the lowest-priced name — spread costs and price impact on entry/exit are disproportionate. Three of the five positions (ATYM, SHAW, BCG) ended negative, all sub-£10 price points, which suggests a systematic bias: the no-filter rule is selecting illiquid, reverting names alongside genuine movers.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.81% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price slipped from entry and never recovered, following the same fade pattern as ATYM and SHAW. The +4.81% prior-day gain — smallest in the basket — did not persist, consistent with a low-float, noise-driven move.
executed.risks_observed: At end-of-day basket level, the two profitable trades (both large-cap, both >11% prior-day gaps) were outweighed by three small-cap fades, resulting in a net-negative session despite a 40% win rate by count. The strategy has no size, liquidity, or market-cap filter, so position sizing is identical across names with very different risk profiles — a structural issue more than a one-day observation.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+5.79% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted marginally higher from entry to scheduled exit, consistent with partial continuation of the prior-day gap — the weakest form of confirmation. The gain is real but thin enough that a slightly wider spread or worse fill flips it.
executed.risks_observed: At £1.99 the bid-ask spread is a meaningful fraction of any achievable intraday move; the strategy has no cost filter to screen out low-price names where round-trip friction dominates. No intraday stop meant the full position was exposed if the reversal had been sharper.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+5.49% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Entry and exit at the same price with a negligible loss; the prior-day gain was fully absorbed at the open with zero intraday follow-through. The thesis found no expression whatsoever on the day.
executed.risks_observed: At £0.33 the percentage spread on a round-trip likely exceeds the nominal P&L figure, meaning this trade was a net loser in real execution terms. Penny-stock names at this price level amplify all cost risks and should be filtered or position-sized differently.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+5.10% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price declined from entry through to the scheduled exit with no recovery — prior-day momentum reversed immediately and the position absorbed the full drawdown. There was no intraday catalyst to suggest a rebound was imminent before the exit triggered.
executed.risks_observed: RR.L is the highest-profile, most institutionally-traded name in this basket; gap-up momentum signals on large-caps are mean-reverting faster than on smaller names, and the rule applies no market-cap filter. Absence of a stop-loss meant the strategy held through the entire adverse move.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.48% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price was unchanged tick-for-tick between entry and exit; the prior-day move was fully digested with no directional resolution during the holding window. The trade generated no information about whether the thesis works on Paris-listed names.
executed.risks_observed: A EUR-denominated position in a GBP-settled book introduces FX basis that can mask real economic moves in either direction; the flat price reading may obscure a small loss after conversion costs. Capital was tied up with zero return and positive transaction cost exposure.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.40% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Best outcome in the basket: price moved cleanly from entry to a higher scheduled exit, with prior-day momentum extending into the session. Small-cap continuation is precisely the regime this rule is trying to exploit.
executed.risks_observed: At £0.64 with a 1.56% gain the spread still consumes a meaningful slice of the return; a marginally worse exit would have compressed this significantly. The gain could reflect thin liquidity amplifying a small order rather than broad demand — hard to distinguish without volume data.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+12.26% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price held exactly at the open level through the scheduled exit, generating no intraday signal. After a 12%+ overnight gap — the largest in this basket — the complete absence of movement is itself informative: either liquidity dried up after the open print or the gap was driven by a one-off event that exhausted buying interest immediately.
executed.risks_observed: A double-digit gap on a UK small-cap is a textbook event-driven move with high mean-reversion risk; entering with a fixed scheduled exit and no stop left the position fully exposed to a fade that never materialised here but will in similar setups. Wide bid/ask spreads at this price and volume level can silently erode effective P&L beyond what the nominal 0.00% suggests.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.31% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit at entry price; the moderate gap held but produced no continuation, leaving the momentum thesis neither confirmed nor refuted. Scheduled exit on a day with zero price movement provides no useful signal about whether the prior session's move had any predictive content.
executed.risks_observed: No stop or profit target means the strategy absorbs intraday drawdowns silently — the flat close could mask a significant intraday excursion that recovered by the scheduled exit time. At the basket level, holding three European names simultaneously (TEP.PA, WKL.AS, CAP.PA) creates latent correlation risk if a macro catalyst hits mid-session.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+3.87% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Entry and scheduled exit both at the same price; the gap stalled immediately with no follow-through. Wolters Kluwer's gap was the smallest of the European names in the basket, and the lack of any movement suggests the prior-session move was already fully absorbed at the open.
executed.risks_observed: WKL.AS correlates closely with CAP.PA and other European data/software names also in this basket, meaning the three Paris/Amsterdam positions are not independent bets — a sector-level sell-off would hit all three simultaneously with no diversification benefit.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+3.82% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Price pinned at the round €100.00 level from entry to scheduled exit with no intraday resolution. The round number likely attracted both buyers and sellers in equal measure, suppressing movement and making this day's data point uninformative for evaluating the momentum rule.
executed.risks_observed: Round-number anchoring at €100 can mask intraday volatility that nets to zero by close; the scheduled exit with no intraday monitoring means the strategy would have sat through any dip and recovery without acting. Capgemini's size means it is index-weight sensitive — any passive rebalancing flow on this date could have driven the temporary stability.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+3.06% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat close at the sub-£1 entry price; the gap failed to extend but also held, giving no directional edge. At this price level, a 0.00% nominal outcome may actually represent a small net loss once spread is accounted for.
executed.risks_observed: Sub-penny spread as a fraction of notional is materially higher for JD.L at £0.72 than for the European names in this basket, making transaction costs a disproportionate headwind that the flat P&L figure obscures. The UK-listed name also introduces GBP/EUR cross-rate noise when evaluating basket-level performance against the two Paris and one Amsterdam positions.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Reads the weekly macro view first; only buys stocks in sectors
the macro agent is bullish on; avoids bearish sectors. Regime-aware top-down
strategy. Region-agnostic — the macro view is global, just the candidate
universe differs per region.

executed.thesis: Agilysys delivered a Q4 double-beat with above-consensus FY27 revenue guidance, driving above-average volume (1.29×) and confirming genuine institutional demand rather than thin-tape drift; Needham reiterates Buy at $120 (34% above close) providing a credible upside anchor. Hospitality software is insulated from the AI-capex-durability concerns dragging semiconductor names, and the earnings catalyst in a risk-off tape marks it as a relative-strength outlier.

ServiceNow's enterprise workflow automation demand is driven by AI-driven efficiency spend — a more durable capex line than infrastructure buildout — separating it from the AMAT/MU/ORCL capex-durability selloff; the stock is above both SMAs with a strong MACD positive crossover after a +22.67% 5-day run. The 65% cash allocation held back reflects the absence of any Energy, Staples, or Healthcare setup that meets both the macro alignment and technical quality bar simultaneously.

Energy is the top macro call for W21 — oil +27.7% (20d) in a stagflationary regime directly inflates offshore drilling day rates, and VAL as a pure-play deepwater/harsh-environment operator is the cleanest expression of sustained high oil prices. RSI 70.3 above both SMAs with positive MACD and +25.79% 20d momentum confirm the sector call is being priced in at the stock level.
executed.outcome_notes: VAL held its upward trajectory through the scheduled exit, with the oil macro tailwind continuing to support offshore drillers without meaningful reversal. The scheduled exit captured the move cleanly — no stop pressure, no thesis deterioration, just trend continuation.
executed.risks_observed: At RSI 70+ on entry, any oil sentiment reversal or inventory surprise could have triggered a sharp mean-reversion in an already extended name. Single-commodity dependency means VAL has near-zero diversification value within an energy-heavy basket.

executed.thesis: Healthcare is rated mildly bullish as defensive rotation re-asserts with quality premium attractive in an uncertain stagflationary regime; BIO has the added stock-level catalyst of Elliott Management building a large activist stake announced today, providing institutional support and a catalyst for operational improvements from a mid-50s RSI that is not yet overbought.
executed.outcome_notes: BIO was the strongest performer in the basket, with the Elliott activist catalyst providing a durable bid that carried through the holding period. Price action confirmed institutional accumulation rather than a fade, validating the event-driven overlay on top of the sector rotation call.
executed.risks_observed: Activist-driven moves can unwind abruptly if the stake is leaked to be smaller than reported, hedged, or if Elliott signals disengagement — the catalyst is binary and not sector-correlated, making it harder to hedge. Sizing this alongside macro defensives creates a mixed-signal position that behaves differently from the rest of the healthcare allocation under stress.

executed.thesis: Technology sector is mildly bullish and oil-price surge creates a secondary narrative tailwind for distributed solar as an alternative-energy beneficiary; ENPH shows the strongest technical confirmation of any Tech candidate with RSI 71.5, +32.72% 5d and +47.49% 20d above both SMAs, with no company-specific negative news to fade the move.
executed.outcome_notes: ENPH effectively went nowhere, with the exit nearly flat despite the strong technical setup on entry — the oil-as-solar-tailwind narrative did not translate into incremental buying during the holding period. The position was dead weight: extended technicals stalled rather than resolved higher.
executed.risks_observed: Entering a name at RSI 71.5 and +47% 20d momentum with a narrative-driven rather than fundamental thesis is a timing risk — momentum exhaustion is the base case, not the edge case. The indirect relationship between oil prices and ENPH demand is too weak a catalyst to sustain a breakout from an already overbought level, and this trade flagged that risk clearly in hindsight.

executed.thesis: Technology is macro-rated bullish on the AI capex supercycle; NVIDIA's fresh 7.8M-share stake in Coherent is a direct, high-conviction catalyst confirming the photonics/optical-interconnect bottleneck narrative. RSI 63.3 and an expanding MACD histogram (+4.68) leave room to run without being overbought, and price is well clear of both SMAs.
executed.outcome_notes: The position was closed at the scheduled time with no price movement captured. The NVIDIA stake catalyst did not generate intraday follow-through during the holding window, suggesting the announcement was already absorbed into price at entry — the signal was real but the timing was late relative to when the market priced it.
executed.risks_observed: Single-catalyst dependence on a disclosed institutional stake creates event-risk concentration: once the filing is public, the edge is thin and the window short. Both COHR and VRT in the same basket share AI-capex exposure, so any macro or sentiment reversal in that theme would have hit both legs simultaneously with no natural hedge.

executed.thesis: Industrials are mildly bullish via reshoring/infrastructure spend, and Vertiv's AI data-centre power and cooling infrastructure gives it a direct co-exposure to the same capex cycle as tech; three analyst upgrades this week (BofA $440, RBC $435, Barclays $412 vs $371 close) confirm the fundamental re-rating. RSI 69.3 and positive MACD support continued momentum with the $440 BofA target as the natural profit objective.
executed.outcome_notes: Closed flat at the scheduled exit, capturing none of the analyst-upgrade re-rating. Three upgrades in a single week often signals a coordinated view that is already partially priced by the time retail and systematic flows arrive; the $440 BofA target remained a valid destination but required a time horizon the scheduled exit could not accommodate.
executed.risks_observed: RSI at 69.3 on entry left almost no buffer before overbought territory — any intraday rotation out of industrials would have pushed the position into the red before the scheduled close, with no stop in place to limit it. Correlation with COHR in the same session means the basket had two correlated AI-infrastructure longs with a combined unhedged sector bet.

executed.thesis: Energy is the strongest sector this week (+6.71% 5d) and the commodity supercycle narrative is gaining traction in news flow; TRGP's midstream infrastructure model delivers energy exposure with stable, fee-based cash flows that are less sensitive to oil price volatility, backed by RSI 69.6, expanding MACD, and the highest volume ratio (1.49x) among energy candidates.
executed.outcome_notes: Exited at the scheduled time with no gain despite entering the week's top-performing sector. A +6.71% five-day sector move often precedes a consolidation day rather than extension; the scheduled exit removed any ability to distinguish between a pause and a reversal, locking in neither profit nor a defensive cut.
executed.risks_observed: Entering a momentum name after a multi-day run means price can sit flat or retrace even when the underlying thesis is intact — the fee-based model reduces oil-price sensitivity but not entry-timing risk. The highest volume ratio in the energy screen (1.49x) could equally indicate distribution rather than accumulation; without a volume-direction check, it was an ambiguous signal.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Reads the weekly macro view first; only buys stocks in sectors
the macro agent is bullish on; avoids bearish sectors. Regime-aware top-down
strategy. Region-agnostic — the macro view is global, just the candidate
universe differs per region.

executed.thesis: Cleanest expression of the Bullish energy macro call: oil +27.7% (20d), Hormuz crisis reshaping global LNG flows, and TotalEnergies expanding Eastern Mediterranean gas exposure all directly support earnings. RSI 56 leaves room to run, MACD just turning positive, and both SMAs confirm the uptrend without the overbought risk seen in other candidates.
executed.outcome_notes: Position closed flat at schedule with zero price movement captured — the macro tailwinds (oil spike, LNG re-routing) did not translate into intraday TTE.PA price action during the hold window. Scheduled exit fired before the thesis had time to express itself; no directional signal confirmed or invalidated.
executed.risks_observed: A scheduled exit with no stop means the position had no downside protection if oil reversed intraday — the 27.7% 20-day run in crude already prices in significant crisis premium, leaving the stock exposed to a sharp unwind on any de-escalation headline. Holding a Hormuz-narrative position through a fixed schedule rather than a catalyst-driven exit increases gap risk around news flow.

executed.thesis: Health care is Mildly Bullish as defensive rotation re-asserts in a stagflationary regime where consumers are squeezed and rate-sensitives are under pressure. Merck KGaA offers quality defensive exposure with an accelerating MACD histogram (+0.843), RSI 56 (ample room), and no overbought risk despite the broader market selling off on oil/yield concerns.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit at schedule; defensive-rotation thesis produced no measurable price response within the hold window. With entry and exit at identical levels, there is no evidence the stagflationary bid for healthcare quality names was active on this session.
executed.risks_observed: Defensive rotation trades are regime-dependent and slow-moving — a scheduled short-duration exit is structurally mismatched to that thesis, creating a pattern where the position absorbs any intraday noise without capturing the multi-day drift it was sized for. Correlation risk with the rest of the basket is low, but the zero-move outcome suggests broader market conditions were not conducive to any of the four positions.

executed.thesis: Financials are Neutral but ABN AMRO carries a clear stock-specific catalyst: a just-reported analyst forecast beat confirmed by 1.79x above-average volume, which distinguishes it from the sector-level drift. The yield-curve steepening (10Y at 4.595%, actively repricing) provides a medium-term NIM tailwind for a bank that is structurally asset-sensitive.
executed.outcome_notes: Catalyst trade (analyst beat + 1.79x volume confirmation) closed flat at schedule with no follow-through. The volume signal did not sustain into a price move, suggesting either the catalyst was already fully priced at entry or liquidity in ABN.AS absorbed the buying without directional resolution.
executed.risks_observed: Volume-confirmed catalyst trades carry the specific risk that the confirming volume *is* the event — once the burst clears, price stalls or fades. Entering after a high-volume beat rather than ahead of it means the informational edge may already be exhausted. A neutral-sector stock-specific trade also lacks a sector tailwind to carry it if the idiosyncratic catalyst loses momentum.

executed.thesis: Technology is Mildly Bullish on the intact AI/industrial capex cycle, and Infineon's European automotive/industrial semiconductor exposure gives it differentiated upside from that thesis versus pure US megacap tech. MACD is strongly positive and expanding (+5.279/+4.669/+0.610); sized smaller to reflect fading 5d sector momentum and the elevated 41.7% 20d run that warrants a wider stop.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit at schedule; the AI/industrial capex thesis and strongly positive MACD setup produced no intraday price action. The 41.7% 20-day run context noted at entry appears to have capped short-term momentum — price consolidated rather than extended.
executed.risks_observed: All four basket positions closed at exactly zero P&L on a scheduled exit, which points to a structural issue: the strategy is generating well-reasoned entries but the exit mechanism (time-based schedule) is not aligned with any of the thesis durations — energy macro, defensive rotation, catalyst follow-through, and momentum continuation all operate on different timescales, none of which are a fixed intraday window. The 41.7% prior run in IFX.DE was flagged at entry as a risk and the flat outcome is consistent with a stock that is extended and digesting gains rather than continuing.

uncommitted.reflection: MACD deterioration + below-SMA confirmation played out; construction cost headwind delivered the predicted mild decline.

uncommitted.rationale: Real estate (mildly bearish macro) +6.3% 5d, -2.5% 20d; RSI 54 neutral; no momentum in weak sector.

uncommitted.reflection: Below-SMA + negative MACD assumed continued weakness; low-volume 5d bounce was enough to neutralise to flat.

uncommitted.reflection: Neutral 20d momentum and balanced RSI-60 confirmed flat — macro headwind and 5d gain cancelled out.

executed.thesis: RSI 24.7 with four major sell-side desks (JPM, BMO, Truist, Canaccord) all maintaining Buy/Outperform — PT cuts reflect model resets, not a change in view. The 16.6% 20-day decline looks like sector-rotation selling into a quality large-cap biotech, a high-probability technical mean-reversion.

RSI 20.7 is extreme technical exhaustion territory: price is 26% below the 20-day MA with zero news to justify the move, making this the purest no-catalyst selloff in the candidate set. Wide stop accounts for the higher ATR-to-price ratio.
RSI 24.8, 21% below 20-day MA, no negative news, and better relative volume (0.20x vs peers) — a clean mean-reversion setup in a stable dental-equipment name with no earnings overhang visible.
RSI 28 with price 15.7% below 20-day MA; the energy sector sell-off on rate/macro concerns appears technical given no NRG-specific negative catalyst, setting up a reversion toward the recent mean.

RSI 28 and 17.4% below 20-day MA with no fundamental negative news; Oshkosh's defense/industrial end markets are intact and the decline looks like macro-driven sector rotation rather than a company-specific issue.

RSI 27.5 and -31.66% 20-day decline while Citigroup maintains a Buy rating signals a technical selloff, not fundamental deterioration — analyst conviction argues the market has overshot. Price at $17.42 vs $22.07 20-day MA implies ~27% reversion to mean.
executed.outcome_notes: Price nudged up 11 cents before the scheduled exit — directionally correct but capturing only a sliver of the ~27% MA gap. No catalyst emerged to accelerate the move, so the Citigroup Buy rating provided a sentiment floor rather than a lift; the thesis held in direction but the hold window was far too short to express a reversion of that magnitude.
executed.risks_observed: A 27% gap to the 20-day MA implies a multi-week recovery horizon that a same-session scheduled exit structurally cannot capture — the mismatch between thesis timeframe and exit rule is the primary inefficiency here. Medical device names at this discount also carry latent binary risk (reimbursement rulings, trial readouts) that a clean RSI oversold signal does not screen for.

executed.thesis: RSI 22.6 with zero news accompanying a -24% 5-day and -29% 20-day move — price is 25% below the 20-day MA with no fundamental reason visible. Textbook technical exhaustion; mean-reversion target is the ~$36–38 MA band.
executed.outcome_notes: Price fell a further 34 cents through the hold, meaning sellers were not exhausted despite the extreme RSI reading. The 'no visible news' framing proved insufficient — either institutional distribution or index-related flow was driving continuation that technical exhaustion signals failed to flag.
executed.risks_observed: A -24%/-29% 5/20-day decline with no public catalyst is a stronger signal of supply overhang than of panic selling; the exhaustion thesis required the selling to be emotional, not structural. The scheduled-exit-only structure had no floor: a deeper continuation trade would have compounded the loss unchecked, and REZI represented the basket's highest open-ended downside exposure.

executed.thesis: RSI 24.2 with no accompanying news after a -20% 20-day decline in a stable auto parts distribution business. Price at $24.67 vs $28.88 20-day MA offers a well-defined ~17% bounce target with no fundamental trigger for the move down.
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted up 6 cents — essentially flat relative to the 17% MA gap the thesis targeted. Auto parts distribution is low-volatility by nature; absent a specific catalyst, mean-reversion in this sector plays out over days to weeks, not hours, so the scheduled exit captured noise rather than signal.
executed.risks_observed: LKQ and CCS share indirect rate-cycle sensitivity through consumer credit and housing turnover, making them more correlated within this basket than their separate sector labels suggest. The near-zero gain came from bid-ask drift, not directional movement, which raises the question of whether transaction costs net this trade to a loss at execution-quality granularity.

executed.thesis: RSI 28.9 and -23% 20-day selloff with no company-specific news — homebuilder weakness is macro rate-fear driven, not CCS-specific. Price at $48.60 vs $55.43 20-day MA provides a clean reversion target once rate panic subsides.
executed.outcome_notes: Price slipped 13 cents, confirming that macro rate pressure on homebuilders continued through the hold window without pause. The thesis required rate panic to abate — it didn't within the session — and the scheduled exit gave no time for that condition to change.
executed.risks_observed: CCS and REZI together concentrate rate-beta risk in the same basket: one a homebuilder, the other HVAC and smart-home, both sensitive to the same rate-fear regime driving the selloffs. Neither position had a stop, so a sustained rate move could have extended both losses simultaneously; the basket carried this correlation without any hedge or sizing adjustment.

executed.thesis: RSI 26.5 oversold in a defensive consumer staple; analyst maintained Equal-Weight (no downgrade) after a -17% 20-day move, confirming no fundamental change. Flowers Foods' predictable business profile and the best volume ratio (0.44) among the deep-oversold names make this the cleanest execution setup.
executed.outcome_notes: Price moved a single cent — the defensive staple's low intraday volatility meant no reversion trigger materialised within the hold window, and no deterioration occurred either. The 'cleanest setup' characterisation held in the sense that it didn't trade against the position, but scheduled exit captured nothing of the underlying thesis.
executed.risks_observed: At a $7.32 handle, the bid-ask spread consumes a disproportionate share of any small gain, making transaction cost drag a structural drag that the P&L number likely flatters. Held alongside REZI and CCS — both with sharper loss potential — FLO's low-volatility profile offers no basket-level offset; it adds neither hedge nor uncorrelated return.

executed.thesis: Cleanest mean-reversion setup: RSI 29.3 (below 30 threshold), price 1.9 ATRs below the 20-day MA at £1.30, and no news-driven catalyst for the -16.8% 20-day decline. Low volume today suggests seller exhaustion rather than continued distribution.
executed.outcome_notes: Price did recover enough to trigger the 'recovered' exit, but the bounce was shallow — the -16.8% 20-day decline absorbed the recovery before price could meaningfully close the gap to SMA20. Low-volume exhaustion read was directionally correct but timing was off; seller pressure persisted into the session.
executed.risks_observed: An entry at the RSI 30 threshold is still well above capitulation territory for a name with a 16.8% drawdown — the 'exhaustion' signal was ambiguous. No stop-loss is visible in the structure, so the position was entirely dependent on the recovered trigger firing before further deterioration.

executed.thesis: Extreme RSI 23.7 with price 3.4 ATRs below SMA20 and no fundamental news to explain the -25.76% 20-day decline; very low volume today (0.13x average) points to technical exhaustion. Smaller allocation accounts for the unusual severity of the decline.
executed.outcome_notes: The 'recovered' exit fired but the loss was the largest in the basket, consistent with a 3.4 ATR extension that rarely snaps back in a single holding period. The 0.13x volume read as exhaustion, but thin volume can also mean no buyers — price drifted rather than bounced.
executed.risks_observed: A 25.76% 20-day decline at RSI 23.7 is well outside normal mean-reversion territory; the reduced allocation acknowledged severity but the setup was closer to a falling-knife than the others. Correlation with other UK small/mid names in the basket amplified downside if any macro catalyst hit the session.

executed.thesis: RSI 35.2 with price 1.8 ATRs below SMA20 and no news catalyst for the -10.76% 20-day pullback; MACD histogram negative but RSI at levels that historically precede mean-reversion bounces.
executed.outcome_notes: The shallowest setup in the basket (RSI 35.2, 1.8 ATRs) produced the smallest loss, suggesting the signal was calibrated correctly relative to the other entries — the recovery path was cleaner with less extension to unwind. Exit on 'recovered' captured a real, if modest, bounce.
executed.risks_observed: RSI 35.2 is borderline for this strategy's threshold and the MACD histogram still negative at entry means momentum had not yet turned — the trade relied entirely on RSI reversion without confirming price action. Liquidity in MGNS.L may be thin enough that the exit itself moved the price.

executed.thesis: RSI 36.8 and 2.2 ATRs below SMA20 with no company-specific negative news; defensive utility characteristics reduce falling-knife risk relative to cyclical names, making this a lower-risk mean-reversion entry.
executed.outcome_notes: The defensive utility framing held in the sense that the loss was contained and the recovered exit fired, but SSE's 2.2 ATR extension didn't fully close — the bounce was real but not enough to overcome entry friction. Sector characteristics limited downside without accelerating the upside.
executed.risks_observed: Utility names carry regulatory and rate-sensitivity risk that RSI/ATR models don't capture; a rate-driven selloff looks identical to a mean-reversion setup but doesn't reverse on technicals alone. The basket held four UK-listed names simultaneously, concentrating currency and UK-macro exposure.

executed.thesis: RSI 35.1 and 2.2 ATRs below SMA20 with no stock-specific bad news; the -11% 20-day decline appears sector/macro-driven (tariff overhang) rather than fundamental deterioration, making a technical bounce plausible as sentiment stabilises.
executed.outcome_notes: The tariff overhang identified in the thesis remained live — sentiment did not stabilise on the day, so the technical bounce was weak and the recovered trigger caught only a shallow move. A macro-driven decline in an auto name rarely reverses on intraday technicals unless the macro catalyst itself resolves.
executed.risks_observed: Entering a EUR-denominated name into a GBP P&L book adds FX drag that isn't reflected in the RSI/ATR signal; any EUR weakness on the day compounded the loss. Sector-level tariff risk is a regime shift, not a reversion candidate — this setup had higher false-signal probability than the domestic UK names in the same basket.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (GAMAl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted ~1% higher from entry to the scheduled exit, consistent with a mild mean-reversion recovery. The move was orderly with no intraday whipsaw implied by the clean close above entry; the thesis held in the sense that price returned toward recent range without needing a stop.
executed.risks_observed: Reconciled entry means the original signal quality is unknown — the position could have been entered at any point in the day's range, making it impossible to assess whether the reversion trigger was well-timed. No intraday stop data means downside exposure during the hold is unverifiable.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (AEPl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: Price slipped 5p from entry before the scheduled exit closed the position flat-to-small-loss; the expected reversion did not materialise, or the entry was already at fair value with no catalyst to pull price back. The scheduled exit prevented further drift but left no opportunity for recovery.
executed.risks_observed: Missing original entry context makes it unclear whether this was a genuine oversold signal or a position carried from a prior session with an already-exhausted thesis; without a stop-loss floor, the position was fully exposed to any further leg lower through the hold window.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (WIXl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: Entry and exit prices are identical at £1.73, so the P&L gain likely reflects FX conversion or a bid/ask spread advantage rather than any directional move; the stock effectively went nowhere and the scheduled exit captured noise-level positive slippage.
executed.risks_observed: At sub-£2 penny-stock prices, the bid/ask spread is proportionally large and can dominate realised P&L in either direction; a one-tick adverse fill at entry or exit would have flipped this to a loss, and the flat price action offers no confirmation the mean-reversion signal was valid.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (IFXd_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: IFX.DE fell over 2.4% from entry to the scheduled exit with no recovery — the position moved against the thesis throughout the hold and the scheduled exit crystallised the full drawdown without any mean-reversion occurring. Given Infineon's sensitivity to semiconductor cycle sentiment, a sector-level sell-off or macro negative likely drove the sustained directional move rather than idiosyncratic noise.
executed.risks_observed: This is the session's worst outcome and highlights the absence of a stop-loss: a 2.4% adverse move in a liquid semiconductor name is well within normal daily range for IFX.DE, meaning the strategy had no mechanism to cut before the scheduled exit. Combined with ABN.AS also losing on the day, continental European exposure contributed disproportionately to the basket's downside.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (SSPGl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: Price ticked up one pip to exit at £1.56, producing a small gain consistent with a minimal mean-reversion in a thinly-priced stock; the move is too small to attribute to any specific driver and is within normal noise for a sub-£2 name.
executed.risks_observed: Liquidity risk is the primary concern at this price level — small order flow can move the mid-price materially, and the reconciled entry raises questions about fill quality; the gain could easily reverse sign with a slightly different exit timing.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (PAGEl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: PAGE.L was the session's strongest performer, gaining ~1.5% from entry to the scheduled exit; PageGroup as a recruitment-sector stock can move on macro employment data or sector rotation, and the clean upside move suggests either a genuine reversion from a prior oversold level or a day when risk appetite favoured cyclical UK mid-caps.
executed.risks_observed: The scheduled exit left money on the table if the move had legs beyond the session, but more relevantly it also means no trailing stop captured the gain dynamically — the outcome depended entirely on the arbitrary end-of-day timing. Recruitment stocks are high-beta to economic sentiment, so the same catalyst that drove this gain could reverse sharply.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (ABNa_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: ABN AMRO dropped ~1.6% from entry to the scheduled exit with no recovery; European bank names are sensitive to rate expectations and credit spreads, and a sustained directional decline of this magnitude suggests a macro or sector headwind rather than the temporary dislocation mean-reversion targets.
executed.risks_observed: ABN.AS and IFX.DE both lost meaningfully on the same day, indicating correlated continental European exposure that amplified the basket's downside — two of the three losers are non-UK names, which is a concentration risk if the strategy is not explicitly sizing for cross-border correlation. No stop on either position compounded the effect.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (SSEl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: SSE gained 1.3% from entry to the scheduled exit, performing as a defensive utility would in a mixed-market session where investors rotated toward lower-beta names; the move was directional and steady rather than a sharp reversion, which may reflect sector tailwinds rather than pure mean-reversion dynamics.
executed.risks_observed: SSE's positive performance on a day when European cyclicals (IFX.DE, ABN.AS) sold off suggests this position benefited from a flight-to-defensives dynamic that is not repeatable on mean-reversion logic alone; relying on this name as a 'safe' basket component could mask sector-rotation dependency that the strategy does not model.

executed.thesis: Reconciled from T212 portfolio (BABl_EQ) — original ledger entry missing
executed.outcome_notes: BAB.L ticked up ~0.8% to the scheduled exit, a modest gain consistent with mild reversion in a low-volatility defence/engineering name; the move is within normal daily range and gives no strong signal about whether the entry thesis was correct.
executed.risks_observed: At the basket level, the session's P&L was dominated by two significant losers (IFX.DE, ABN.AS) against several small UK winners; the small gains across GAMA.L, WIX.L, SSPG.L, PAGE.L, SSE.L, and BAB.L did not fully offset the continental European drawdowns, pointing to an implicit geographic tilt in position sizing that warrants review.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Buys recent winners that are still trending. Region-agnostic
bias — the same prompt runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and against
UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The LLM is told the universe is
whatever's been pre-filtered for it and adapts to the regime.

executed.thesis: Textbook post-earnings momentum entry: Q4 beat with strong FY27 revenue guidance, volume at 1.40x average confirming institutional buying, Needham reiterating Buy at $120 (~32% above current). The only candidate with all momentum signals aligned — catalyst, volume, trend, and analyst support simultaneously present.

Back-to-back analyst PT raises (Barclays $650, Keybanc $700 vs $622 current) provide a fresh catalyst to justify the extension past RSI 85; cybersecurity is the one tech sub-sector insulated from the broader XLK 5-day crack given the AI cyber-risk demand tailwind.
Momentum stub pick. 5-day return +14.74%, RSI 70.7 (in healthy 50–75 band), price above 20-day MA. Bracket: -3.0% stop / +5.0% take-profit.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Buys recent winners that are still trending. Region-agnostic
bias — the same prompt runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and against
UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The LLM is told the universe is
whatever's been pre-filtered for it and adapts to the regime.

executed.thesis: Cleanest momentum trade in the list — Infineon's +11%/+34.5% 5d/20d returns, non-overbought RSI (65.9), and strongly positive MACD reflect genuine demand for power semiconductors in AI data-centre buildouts; stop set at ~2x ATR below close, target at ~3x ATR.
executed.outcome_notes: Price moved against the position before recovering to the exit level, suggesting the stop was either triggered or the system exited on the partial recovery rather than holding for target. The AI data-centre demand narrative remained intact structurally, but the near-term action implies either profit-taking off the recent run or broader semiconductor sector rotation on the day — not a thesis invalidation.
executed.risks_observed: The +34.5% 20-day move meant significant embedded gains in the float; any macro wobble or sector rotation can flush late momentum entrants even when the thesis is sound. The missing entry price ($0.00) is a data integrity gap that makes it impossible to verify whether the stop was sized correctly relative to realised volatility on the day.

executed.thesis: ABN AMRO just beat Q1 forecasts with analyst upgrades following; +11% 5d with accelerating MACD histogram and yield curve steepening providing a structural NIM tailwind for European bank margins — fundamental catalyst reinforcing the technical trend.
executed.outcome_notes: The trade exited at a loss despite the price recovering, indicating the recovery was incomplete — the exit was taken on the bounce rather than at breakeven or target. European bank momentum is highly sensitive to intraday macro signals (rate prints, ECB commentary), and any reversal in short-end rates or credit spreads on the day would have immediately pressured NIM-driven names regardless of the Q1 catalyst.
executed.risks_observed: All three basket positions were in trending, recently strong sectors simultaneously — semiconductors, banks, and consumer staples — meaning a broad risk-off intraday move would hit all legs at once with no natural hedge. ABN specifically adds FX exposure (EUR/GBP) that is unaccounted for in the exit price, which may have contributed to the sterling-denominated loss even if the local price recovered.

executed.thesis: British American Tobacco is the clearest expression of the macro view's Consumer Staples defensive bid — +17% 20d, above both MAs, strong MACD momentum; position sized below max given RSI at 70.0 approaching overbought.
executed.outcome_notes: RSI was flagged at exactly 70.0 at entry, and the recovered exit with a loss is consistent with a brief overbought pullback that failed to fully retrace — the defensive/staples bid provided some cushion but not enough to prevent a negative close. The smaller loss relative to the other two legs suggests the reduced position sizing on overbought concern functioned as intended.
executed.risks_observed: At RSI 70.0, any catalyst for profit-taking (currency move, sector rotation into risk-on) creates asymmetric downside for late momentum entries; the strategy has no mechanism to distinguish between a healthy consolidation and a trend reversal at that signal level. Basket-level, all three trades sharing a 'recovered but still negative' outcome on the same date points to a common intraday driver — worth checking whether a single macro event (e.g. USD/EUR move, risk-off spike) explains the simultaneous drawdown across uncorrelated sectors.

executed.thesis: Strongest 5-day return among non-overbought candidates (+14.18%), rising volume (1.14x), and RSI 68.9 leaves room before the danger zone; defensive tobacco is directly aligned with the macro stagflationary rotation favouring consumer staples over rate-sensitives.
executed.outcome_notes: Scheduled close returned exactly zero, meaning price went nowhere from entry to exit — the 5-day momentum in BATS.L had no intraday continuation on the entry session. The defensive-rotation catalyst was either already fully priced at entry or lacked a fresh trigger to push it further on the day.
executed.risks_observed: Entering a name already up 14%+ over five days on a session that delivered flat price action is a warning sign that momentum was exhausted at the point of entry; the scheduled-exit structure means the position couldn't benefit from any multi-day follow-through while still bearing overnight gap risk. All four basket positions closed flat on the same day, suggesting a systemic timing issue rather than a stock-specific one.

executed.thesis: Top 5-day return in the entire candidate set (+14.36%) with price extended well above both MAs and expanding MACD histogram; RSI 74.2 is borderline overbought so stop is set at 1.5× ATR to protect against mean-reversion while the trend runs.
executed.outcome_notes: Zero P&L on a scheduled close; ITRK.L entered with the highest-ranked 5-day return in the set and an RSI already at 74.2, and price action on the entry day produced no extension whatsoever. The momentum signal appears to have been lagging rather than leading — the move had run before entry.
executed.risks_observed: An RSI of 74.2 is the clearest mean-reversion risk in this basket and a scheduled (likely same-day) close window gives insufficient time for trend momentum to overcome the overbought condition; the 1.5× ATR stop was appropriately defensive but the reward setup requires immediate continuation that didn't arrive. This was the highest-risk entry in the basket on the overbought dimension.

executed.thesis: Highest volume ratio in the set (1.79×) confirms institutional accumulation on the back of a Q1 earnings beat and analyst estimate upgrades; RSI 68.0 with a healthy MACD histogram provides clean technical backing for continuation.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit at scheduled close; the high volume ratio (1.79×) that justified an institutional-accumulation read did not translate into price continuation on the entry day, suggesting the Q1 earnings catalyst had already been absorbed by the market prior to entry. Post-earnings momentum entries are sensitive to exactly this timing risk.
executed.risks_observed: ABN.AS introduces European financials exposure that behaves differently from the UK consumer and industrials names in the same basket — credit spread widening or ECB commentary can move it independently and in the opposite direction. The earnings-beat thesis has a short shelf life; entering days after the catalyst leaves only technical momentum to carry the trade, which failed here.

executed.thesis: Robust 20-day trend (+16.62%) with above-average volume (1.43×) and RSI 66.3 leaving meaningful headroom before overbought; wider stop accounts for the larger ATR-relative spread typical of lower-priced shares while the 2:1 reward ratio keeps expected value positive.
executed.outcome_notes: Scheduled close at entry price with no movement captured; the strong 20-day trend in CPG.L at a £0.32 handle meant the bid-ask spread likely consumed any marginal intraday drift, and the entry session produced no directional push to overcome that friction. The trend signal was real but the holding window was too short to express it.
executed.risks_observed: At £0.32 per share, the spread is a material drag on the stated 2:1 reward ratio — the effective entry is worse than the mid-price and the effective exit is worse still, meaning the break-even threshold is structurally higher than the model assumes. The wider ATR-adjusted stop needed for low-priced shares also limits position sizing, reducing this name's contribution to basket returns even when the directional call is correct.

uncommitted.reflection: RSI=77 overbought caveat proved correct — 5-day momentum stalled and flipped to a marginal loss rather than extending.

uncommitted.reflection: Post-bid RSI=80.9 unwind exceeded flat forecast as deal uncertainty emerged exactly as the downside scenario described.
