uncommitted.rationale: RSI 29.3 with no reversal signal, -16.8% on 20d, consumer discretionary headwinds from macro (XLY mildly bearish).
uncommitted.reflection: Downtrend direction correct; RSI=29.3 near-oversold absorbed some selling, delivering roughly half the forecast decline.

uncommitted.rationale: Steady 11% 5-day gain with neutral RSI=64; no overbought risk
uncommitted.reflection: Clean RSI=64 setup failed to follow through — 5-day gain reversed without any overbought trigger to explain the stall.

uncommitted.rationale: Consistent 10% losses across both periods; downtrend confirmed
uncommitted.reflection: Downtrend correctly identified; consistent loss pattern accelerated sharply beyond the -0.70% target.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent bounce (10% 5-day) not supported by flat 20-day trend

uncommitted.reflection: RSI=73 assessed as healthy, not overbought; reversal suggests exhaustion arrived earlier than the rationale judged.

uncommitted.rationale: 9% 5-day gain with RSI=77 overbought; pullback risk
uncommitted.reflection: Upside direction correct despite RSI=77 pullback warning — momentum carried 4x beyond the conservative forecast.

uncommitted.rationale: 9% 5-day bounce contradicted by 13% 20-day loss
uncommitted.reflection: Contradictory 5-day/20-day signals correctly resolved to flat — neither trend prevailed.

uncommitted.rationale: Accelerating selloff with 9% 5-day drop adding to 14% 20-day loss
uncommitted.reflection: Accelerating selloff direction correct; drop ran 4x forecast as momentum deterioration proved deeper than modelled.

uncommitted.rationale: 9% 5-day gain with RSI=60 healthy; modest trend
uncommitted.reflection: RSI=60 healthy trend read correctly but 'modest' label missed a breakout — gain was nearly 9x the predicted magnitude.

uncommitted.rationale: 9% 5-day gain with dividend narrative supporting RSI=69
uncommitted.reflection: Dividend narrative and RSI=69 momentum read correctly — gain doubled forecast as supportive conditions held.

uncommitted.rationale: Analyst revenue upgrade today is a positive catalyst but underlying trend is -23% on 20d with deeply negative MACD — news-driven bounce at best, not a momentum trade.
uncommitted.reflection: Analyst upgrade catalyst underweighted — news-driven bounce far exceeded what the negative 20-day/MACD baseline implied.

uncommitted.rationale: 9% 5-day bounce on barely positive 20-day trend (0.5%)
uncommitted.reflection: Thin 20-day trend correctly flagged the 5-day bounce as fragile — stock gave back gains and dipped beyond flat.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Weights earnings surprises, M&A, guidance changes, and other
catalysts. News routing per ticker: Alpaca News for US-listed names,
yfinance fallback for UK/EU listings — coverage is decent everywhere.

executed.thesis: Agilysys filed an 8-K on May 18 reporting a Q4 double beat plus FY27 revenue guidance above analyst estimates, the archetypal earnings-catalyst setup for this strategy. Volume is running at 1.28× the 20-day average today, confirming the market is still absorbing the news rather than already fully pricing it; Needham reiterates Buy at $120 (32% above current price), providing analyst-backed upside runway.

Publicis Groupe announced an all-cash acquisition of LiveRamp via a definitive merger agreement filed in an 8-K today (May 18); stock is trading at M&A arb discount to likely deal price (~$40) with 8x average volume confirming sustained institutional positioning in deal spread.
executed.outcome_notes: The M&A spread failed to compress intraday — the stock drifted fractionally below entry and held its discount to the ~$40 deal price through close. High volume confirmed institutional awareness of the spread, but that positioning was already reflected in the opening price, leaving no intraday compression to capture on a same-day exit.
executed.risks_observed: The thesis is structurally a multi-week arb, not an intraday catalyst play; a scheduled same-day exit is mismatched to the convergence timeline of a definitive merger agreement. Deal-break risk (regulatory, financing, competing bidder absence) is also unhedged, and any adverse headline would gap the stock well below entry with no stop in place.

executed.thesis: WSJ reported today that Elliott Management built a large activist stake in Bio-Rad; activist involvement is a strong and fresh catalyst for strategic action and multiple expansion, with RSI 55.8 leaving significant room before overbought conditions.
executed.outcome_notes: The Elliott activist disclosure drove a clean, sustained bid through the session with no meaningful intraday reversal before the scheduled exit. Price action was consistent with institutional accumulation continuing after the initial news, validating the catalyst quality.
executed.risks_observed: Activist-stake disclosures can be front-run by prime brokerage desks before public reporting, so entry at open may already be late relative to the initial move. If the position is part of a basket with other activist-catalyst names, correlation spikes on broad risk-off days could compress the spread before strategic action materialises.

executed.thesis: BofA Securities reinstated ServiceNow as Buy with a $130 price target today (29% implied upside), stock is above both key moving averages with elevated relative volume of 0.65 suggesting institutional follow-through on the fresh analyst catalyst.
executed.outcome_notes: The BofA reinstatement generated consistent follow-through buying into close, with the stock moving ~1.9% — the large implied upside in the price target drew momentum participants alongside fundamental buyers. Exit at close captured the full intraday move without reversion.
executed.risks_observed: Analyst reinstatements with wide price targets are frequently anticipated by sell-side desks ahead of publication, meaning a portion of the move may have already occurred before the open entry. With a scheduled exit and no stop, any intraday reversal — e.g., from a competing downgrade or sector sell-off — would have been held through in full.

executed.thesis: Benchmark raised Intel's price target to $140 today alongside Trump publicly signaling political support for Intel investment; after a -17.6% 5-day pullback from a massive +62% 20-day run, the stock has reset to a more favorable entry above both MAs.
executed.outcome_notes: The stock was flat on the session despite two fresh catalysts, with the price target raise from a secondary broker and political commentary failing to attract net buyers on the day. Post-run distribution pressure from the prior +62% move appears to have fully absorbed the catalyst flow.
executed.risks_observed: Entering after a parabolic 20-day run into a Benchmark (not tier-1) price target raise carries significant overhead supply risk; the thesis required momentum continuation but the prior move likely exhausted near-term buyers. Political signals are unreliable as price catalysts with no defined timeline, introducing further uncertainty about when or whether the thesis would resolve.

executed.thesis: NVIDIA initiated a 7.79M-share position in Coherent Corp (filed 2026-05-15), a direct institutional endorsement of COHR's role in AI optical networking infrastructure. RSI 63.3 is not overbought, volume is at-average (1.0x) rather than exhausted, suggesting the market is still absorbing the NVIDIA signal rather than having already fully priced it.
executed.outcome_notes: Price was unchanged from entry to scheduled exit, indicating the NVIDIA filing (disclosed 2026-05-15, two days before entry) had already been absorbed by the market before the position was opened. No directional follow-through materialised during the hold window.
executed.risks_observed: Entering two calendar days after the catalyst filing is the core failure mode here — the news-to-entry lag was long enough for institutional fast-money to have already acted. This position was also duplicated in the same session (see 4b7a7798), doubling COHR concentration on an identical signal without any new information.

executed.thesis: Three simultaneous analyst upgrades with raised price targets on 2026-05-15 (B of A to $440, Barclays to $412, RBC to $435 vs current $370.94) represent coordinated sell-side conviction in Vertiv's AI data-centre infrastructure story; consensus targets imply 11–19% upside from here with RSI 69.3 leaving room before overbought territory.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit at the scheduled time with no intraday price response to the triple-upgrade cluster, which had also been published two days prior. The window between catalyst and entry was again sufficient for the market to fully reprice, leaving no edge.
executed.risks_observed: Same-day duplicate position in VRT (228912b0) created unintended 2x concentration in an already RSI-elevated name; if the stock had reversed, losses would have been doubled. VRT and COHR are both AI-infrastructure plays held simultaneously, adding sector-level correlation to the basket.

executed.thesis: NVIDIA disclosed a new 7,788,161-share strategic stake in Coherent Corp on 2026-05-15 — a direct endorsement from the dominant AI-infrastructure company for its optical-transceiver supplier; earnings risk is already past (reported 2026-05-06) and RSI at 63 signals the market is still digesting the news rather than fully pricing it.
executed.outcome_notes: Identical outcome to 6ad9bcf1 — flat scheduled exit on the same ticker, same price, same session. The position added no incremental exposure beyond the duplicate; it effectively just doubled notional on a signal that had already gone stale.
executed.risks_observed: Duplicate-firing on the same catalyst is a strategy-level defect: the deduplication guard either failed or is absent. Running two full-size positions in COHR simultaneously inflated single-name concentration to levels that would have been painful had a sharp adverse move occurred.

executed.thesis: BofA ($440), Barclays ($412), and RBC ($435) all raised price targets on the same day with Vertiv at $370.94, well below all three consensus targets; a same-day triple-upgrade cluster from top-tier banks is a concrete, positive catalyst and the 9% five-day move leaves meaningful room to the consensus PT range.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat at scheduled exit, mirroring 09459e9c exactly. No incremental price discovery occurred during the hold, consistent with a catalyst that had been fully discounted before the position was initiated.
executed.risks_observed: The basket on this date carried four positions across only two AI-infrastructure names (COHR ×2, VRT ×2) plus one energy name, meaning roughly 80% of deployed capital was correlated to the same AI data-centre theme. A single adverse macro headline (rate spike, export-control news) could have hit both names simultaneously.

executed.thesis: A confluence of oil-specific macro catalysts — Jeff Currie's commodity supercycle call, Bessent's Hormuz reopening signal, and reports of well-timed pre-announcement oil bets — is driving volume 1.68x the 20-day average into XOM with RSI at 59.6 still below overbought, suggesting institutional positioning in progress.
executed.outcome_notes: Exited flat at the scheduled time despite above-average volume on entry, suggesting the elevated volume reflected broad market participation rather than sustained directional conviction in XOM specifically. The macro catalysts cited (Currie call, Hormuz signal) are slow-moving and may not generate a single-session price move.
executed.risks_observed: Macro-narrative catalysts (supercycle thesis, geopolitical signals) are inherently diffuse and hard to time — they argue for a multi-day hold, not an intraday or short scheduled window, so the exit structure was mismatched to the thesis. XOM provided sector diversification from the AI names but was the only energy position, leaving the strategy's non-AI leg thin.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Weights earnings surprises, M&A, guidance changes, and other
catalysts. News routing per ticker: Alpaca News for US-listed names,
yfinance fallback for UK/EU listings — coverage is decent everywhere.

executed.thesis: Confirmed $3.875bn divestiture of Australian steelmaking coal assets is a hard M&A catalyst completing Anglo's portfolio simplification ahead of Teck merger; analyst fair value revised to £36.53 and ESG-driven re-rating thesis has further to run as restricted capital returns.
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted below entry throughout the hold and exited at the scheduled time with no recovery. The divestiture announcement was already digested by the market at entry — notably, the revised analyst fair value of £36.53 sat below the entry price of £37.07, leaving no valuation buffer for the thesis to play out.
executed.risks_observed: Entry was priced above the freshly revised fair value, so the risk/reward was inverted from the start. The ESG re-rating and capital returns angle is a multi-week catalyst at best; in a scheduled short-hold structure it generated no price support, and there was no secondary trigger to force a re-rate within the window.

executed.thesis: Yesterday's H2 earnings confirmed return to growth with higher sales, revenue and backlog after a prolonged contraction — reaction is fresh (one day old) and analyst model updates should provide continued tailwind above both SMAs.
executed.outcome_notes: Price was essentially flat at exit, confirming that the earnings-driven momentum had already exhausted itself before entry. Anticipated analyst model updates either didn't arrive during the hold or had no visible price impact — the stock simply consolidated rather than continuing higher.
executed.risks_observed: Entering a full day after the initial reaction means the reactive leg had already run; the position was exposed to post-event drift with no fresh catalyst to sustain it. The near-zero move is the benign outcome — the same timing lag that produced a -0.18% loss here could produce a sharper mean-reversion if the tape turns.

executed.thesis: Confirmed earnings beat (May 17) with shares up 3.6% on results day and drift continuing; post-earnings analyst revisions are still flowing and technicals above both SMAs support further appreciation.
executed.outcome_notes: The stop was triggered, meaning post-earnings drift reversed rather than continued — the earnings beat had been fully absorbed two days prior and offered no ongoing buying pressure. The stop exit at £2.01 against a £2.09 entry implies the stock broke back below the post-results support level, invalidating the technical leg of the thesis.
executed.risks_observed: At this price and size, bid-ask spread and thin liquidity likely widened the effective loss versus the theoretical stop level. The -4.00% drawdown is the largest in today's basket and points to the stop being placed too close to a volatile, low-priced name where normal intraday noise can clip a position; a volatility-adjusted stop or wider hold tolerance is needed for names in this price tier.

executed.thesis: ABN AMRO confirmed an analyst earnings beat on 2026-05-16, reversing prior misses; price is up 9.4% over 5 days on 1.79× average volume, indicating institutional re-rating is still in progress with next earnings not until August — classic post-beat drift setup.
executed.outcome_notes: Price went nowhere between entry and the scheduled close, returning to the exact entry print. The post-beat drift either had already exhausted itself in the prior 5-day run before this session opened, or failed to extend on the day — the scheduled exit gave no room to distinguish between the two.
executed.risks_observed: Entering after a 9.4% / 5-day move creates late-entry timing risk: the institutional re-rating may have already cleared rather than being mid-process. A time-based exit with no intraday profit target means any brief directional spike was left uncaptured, and there was no stop to limit downside had price reversed instead of going flat.

executed.thesis: Fresh Strait of Hormuz crisis coverage (2026-05-17) plus a new Eastern Mediterranean deep-offshore gas MOU with Egypt give TotalEnergies a dual catalyst — supply disruption premium and strategic gas expansion — that has not yet fully lifted volume off its below-average base, leaving room for catch-up.
executed.outcome_notes: Neither the Hormuz supply-risk premium nor the Egypt MOU translated into price movement during the holding window — the stock closed at exactly its entry level. Below-average volume at entry, flagged as a potential catch-up signal, instead indicated lack of conviction; the catalysts may have been too diffuse or already partially priced into the energy complex broadly.
executed.risks_observed: Geopolitical headline trades are highly correlated with sector-wide energy moves, so if crude and the broader E&P basket didn't re-rate on the day, a single-name long had no independent lift. The dual-catalyst framing also risks overfitting to narrative — two simultaneous news drivers can mask the absence of a clean, measurable edge on either one.

uncommitted.reflection: Assumed negative 5d momentum would persist; price reversed sharply, disproving the continuation thesis.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent up, longer flat; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: No-catalyst flat call confirmed; small uptrend continued at a negligible pace.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 5.8% 5d; momentum negative.
uncommitted.reflection: Negative momentum continuation confirmed; loss ran deeper than predicted.

uncommitted.rationale: Small steady up; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Predicted flat on steady uptrend; no catalyst left it vulnerable to mild selling.

uncommitted.rationale: Steady small up; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Predicted flat on steady uptrend; no catalyst allowed mild selling to dominate.

uncommitted.rationale: Mixed signals; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Mixed-signal flat call; absence of a catalyst let price drift lower without support.

uncommitted.rationale: Negative news (premiumization pressures) contradicts small up move; pressure will reassert.
uncommitted.reflection: Premiumization-pressure news expected to weigh; price absorbed the negative news and held flat.

uncommitted.rationale: Small up move; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: No-catalyst flat call confirmed; price drifted fractionally positive within noise.

uncommitted.rationale: Recovering from 41% annual decline with price above both SMAs and positive MACD, but today's valuation commentary is not a hard catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Technical setup above SMAs with positive MACD confirmed; soft catalyst drove a move beyond the mild-up target.

uncommitted.rationale: Uber stake news but 'no takeover' reads mixed; small move, unclear direction.
uncommitted.reflection: Uber stake news read as mixed; market resolved it positively, driving a clean upside move.

uncommitted.rationale: Valuation talk, small recent move; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Soft valuation commentary dismissed as no hard catalyst; market acted on it, driving a strong upside surprise.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 5% 5d; momentum negative.
uncommitted.reflection: Momentum-down assumption missed a sharp mean-reversion; the 5d selloff proved exhaustion, not continuation.

uncommitted.rationale: M&A catalyst already priced in 20d=+14% move; RSI=72 exhausted.
uncommitted.reflection: RSI-72 exhaustion read correctly; post-M&A drift stayed contained and flat as expected.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent up, longer down; mixed, no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Mixed-signals flat call understated residual upward bias; no catalyst but mild drift higher prevailed.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 5% 5d, RSI=36 oversold but no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Oversold RSI-36 cushioned further decline; expected bearish continuation stalled at support.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent up, longer down; mixed signals.
uncommitted.reflection: Longer-term downtrend in the mixed-signals read prevailed over the short-term uptick.

uncommitted.rationale: Macro news (Iran-US talks), RSI=70 overbought; catalyst exhausted.
uncommitted.reflection: Overbought RSI-70 exhaustion read correct; macro catalyst faded and stock held flat as expected.

uncommitted.rationale: MACD sharply negative with RSI 41; -14.5% 20-day trend shows persistent selling and no identifiable catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Persistent-selling assumption met oversold stabilisation; downside capped short of the mild-down target.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 5% 5d, down 10% 20d; RSI=40, momentum down.
uncommitted.reflection: Momentum-down read stalled; broader market bid absorbed the selling pressure and kept the stock flat.

uncommitted.rationale: Pre-earnings uncertainty (May 28) in a downtrend; RSI 37 below both SMAs with no near-term positive catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Pre-earnings downtrend logic sound but oversold RSI halted the move just inside the flat band.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 4.9% 5d; momentum negative.
uncommitted.reflection: Negative 5d momentum confirmed; continued selling delivered the mild-down outcome as expected.

uncommitted.rationale: Generic roundup mention, not company-specific catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Generic roundup correctly signalled no single-stock catalyst, but unmodelled sector tailwind drove the gain.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent small up, 20d down 17% but RSI=40; no catalyst for reversal.
uncommitted.reflection: RSI-40 oversold bounce from a deep 20d drawdown provided the reversal the no-catalyst read dismissed.

uncommitted.rationale: Mixed 5d/20d; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Mixed 5d/20d and no catalyst assumed neutrality; an unidentified demand driver pushed a 3% breakout.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 4.7% 5d; momentum negative.
uncommitted.reflection: Expected momentum continuation from 5d decline; stock reversed sharply, revealing the dip as a mean-reversion setup.

uncommitted.rationale: Small steady up; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: No catalyst flat correct; small steady upward drift failed to sustain and settled flat.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent small up, longer down; mixed signals.
uncommitted.reflection: Mixed-signals flat call missed the longer-term downtrend accelerating into a strong 4.8% drop.

uncommitted.rationale: Mixed signals; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Flat on mixed signals; an unmodelled catalyst drove a near-5% surge the no-catalyst read couldn't capture.

uncommitted.rationale: Analyst price target reductions (1,800p target implied) offset the mild positive drift; no new hard catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Analyst target reductions correctly offset mild positive drift; stock stayed range-bound as expected.

uncommitted.rationale: Negligible fair-value tweak (£31.63 → £31.28) is not a tradeable catalyst; stock is range-bound between SMAs.
uncommitted.reflection: Range-bound SMA read assumed flat; negligible catalyst was right, but range support broke to the downside.

uncommitted.rationale: Steady small up; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Steady-up bias assumed continuation; unidentified selling pressure reversed direction below the flat threshold.

uncommitted.rationale: Recent small up, longer down; mixed.
uncommitted.reflection: Longer-term downtrend reasserted over the short-term uptick, pushing well past the flat call.

uncommitted.rationale: No catalyst, RSI 34, below both SMAs — downtrend continues by default.
uncommitted.reflection: Downtrend default expected further decline; oversold RSI-34 limited the move to flat rather than extending lower.

uncommitted.rationale: Strong move already complete (20d=+13.8%); catalyst exhausted.
uncommitted.reflection: Catalyst-exhausted flat call missed modest extension of the 20d surge; overbought read understated residual momentum.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 4.4% 5d, RSI=36 oversold but no bounce catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Oversold RSI-36 without bounce catalyst assumed continued selling; mean-reversion buyers emerged and flattened the drop.

uncommitted.rationale: Generic materials roundup; no specific catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Generic materials roundup correctly signalled no specific upside; sector selling pushed below the flat call.

uncommitted.rationale: Earnings in 2 days introduce binary risk; muted directional signal while market awaits results.
uncommitted.reflection: Pre-earnings binary caution muted direction correctly; stock drifted up as market leaned constructive ahead of results.

uncommitted.rationale: Energy sector tailwind from WTI above $106 with insider interest noted; above both SMAs but no single-stock catalyst to drive a sharp move.
uncommitted.reflection: Energy tailwind and insider interest anticipated sector lift; single-stock selling overwhelmed the macro bid.

uncommitted.rationale: Small up move; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: No-catalyst flat correct; small upward drift reversed to a marginal decline within noise.

uncommitted.rationale: Small up move; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: No-catalyst flat correct; small upward bias and mild pullback both stayed within the flat band.

uncommitted.rationale: Generic 'undervalued' commentary is not actionable; below SMA20 with no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Below-SMA weakness and no actionable catalyst correctly signalled no upside; stock slipped through the flat floor.

uncommitted.rationale: Small up move; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Flat call missed upside; no-catalyst assumption failed as price found mild bid anyway.

uncommitted.rationale: Small recent up, longer flat; no catalyst.
uncommitted.reflection: Recent-up read assumed continuation; underlying longer-term flat masked building sell pressure.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 4.2% 5d, down 16% 20d, RSI=39 but momentum too negative.
uncommitted.reflection: RSI=39 mean-reversion triggered despite negative momentum, overriding the bearish trend signal.

uncommitted.rationale: Small recent up, big longer down; mixed signals.
uncommitted.reflection: Mixed-signal flat call confirmed; price drifted fractionally positive within noise range.

uncommitted.rationale: Down 4.1% 5d; momentum negative.
uncommitted.reflection: Negative 5d momentum carried through and extended, validating the downside conviction.

uncommitted.rationale: Reebok/O'Neal story is a trend piece not a hard catalyst; positive momentum above both SMAs but limited incremental news.
uncommitted.reflection: Positive momentum above both SMAs stalled; Reebok/O'Neal story priced in, no incremental catalyst to sustain lift.

uncommitted.rationale: Small recent up, longer down; mixed signals.
uncommitted.reflection: Mixed-signal flat assumed equilibrium; mild upside bias in recent leg reasserted against longer-term drag.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Trades sector ETFs based on relative momentum and macro
alignment. US: SPDR sector ETFs (XLF, XLE, XLK, ...). UK/EU: iShares
STOXX Europe 600 sector ETFs (EXH1.DE, EXV3.DE, ...). Same strategy
bias, different ETF universe per region.

executed.thesis: Dominant sector leader by 5d and 20d relative strength, confirmed by MACD histogram expansion, above both SMAs, and a structurally intact oil supply shock thesis with WTI at $106. Wider take-profit to allow the multi-day energy rotation to mature.
Secondary sector leader in a stagflationary regime; above both SMAs with positive MACD and RSI at 60 — defensive pricing power bid is intact as real rates rise and the soft-landing narrative erodes.

Unambiguous sector leader on both 5d (+4.45%) and 20d (+8.43%) timeframes with expanding MACD histogram and healthy RSI 60; the stagflationary macro regime (oil +27.7%) is a direct, durable tailwind that argues for maximum allocation and patient hold.
executed.outcome_notes: Price moved cleanly in the direction of the macro tailwind and the scheduled exit captured the day's drift without any intraday reversal threatening the position. The oil-driven bid held through the session, so the thesis was directionally correct and the full holding period was productive.
executed.risks_observed: Maximum allocation to a single commodity-linked sector creates concentrated exposure to an oil price reversal; a scheduled exit rather than a signal-based one means the strategy would have held through any intraday spike-and-fade without a stop to limit drawdown. On a basket level, XLE dominated returns — if it had reversed, the other two positions were too small to offset it.

executed.thesis: #2 on 5d and #3 on 20d with positively expanding MACD histogram and clean structure above both moving averages; stagflation environments historically rotate into consumer staples quality as real wages erode, and the defensive bid is already showing up in the data.
executed.outcome_notes: The defensive bid materialised but was muted — the move was a fraction of XLE's, suggesting staples participated in a broad risk-on session rather than receiving a distinct flight-to-quality allocation. Scheduled exit left no room to distinguish whether this was the thesis playing out or simply beta to a rising market.
executed.risks_observed: In a day dominated by an energy rally, XLP's correlation to the broader tape means the defensive rationale was not actually tested; a down-tape session is the only valid test of whether the defensive rotation thesis adds independent value. Holding both XLE and XLP simultaneously creates a mild cross-current — one is an inflation beneficiary, the other an inflation hedge — and the portfolio logic breaks down if the macro regime shifts quickly.

executed.thesis: MACD histogram turning up from negative territory (+0.286) while 5d momentum ranks 4th; the macro 'Mildly Bullish' view on defensive rotation into healthcare quality provides a modest but aligned edge — sized smaller given below-SMA50 technical overhang.
executed.outcome_notes: The position was essentially flat — the MACD histogram turn-up produced no meaningful price follow-through on the day, consistent with the below-SMA50 technical overhang suppressing momentum. The smaller sizing was appropriate given the weak signal, but the trade contributed negligible edge.
executed.risks_observed: A position entered below SMA50 with a nascent MACD recovery signal is at risk of being a premature mean-reversion bet disguised as a momentum trade; one more weak session would have invalidated the histogram signal entirely before the scheduled exit. The near-zero contribution also highlights a basket construction risk: three simultaneous scheduled exits create no differentiation between a thesis that confirmed and one that simply didn't move.

executed.thesis: XLE is the clear sector leader on both 5d (+6.71%) and 20d (+8.03%) relative strength, with technicals confirming: RSI 59 (not overbought), bullish MACD histogram, and price well above both SMA20 and SMA50. Higher oil prices are the catalyst this week; macro view is neutral on energy but price action overrides when momentum is this clean.
executed.outcome_notes: The scheduled exit closed the position at exactly the entry price, meaning the oil-driven momentum thesis never converted into realized price movement within the holding window. No directional follow-through developed — either the catalyst was already priced in at entry or the move occurred outside the scheduled hold period.
executed.risks_observed: A flat scheduled exit exposes the strategy's structural dependency on timing: if momentum runs before or after the holding window, the edge is never captured. Single-ETF energy concentration also means any surprise crude inventory print or OPEC headline could have produced an asymmetric loss with no intra-basket offset.

executed.thesis: XLK leads all sectors on 20d relative strength (+14.20%) with macro explicitly bullish on AI capex and semis; elevated volume ratio (1.32) confirms institutional participation. RSI at 73 is stretched so sizing is trimmed and TP widened to let the secular trend run; Friday's rate-fear dip is treated as noise against the dominant uptrend.
executed.outcome_notes: An RSI-73 entry into a rate-fear dip likely stalled the immediate upside, and the scheduled exit locked in a flat result before the secular AI/semis trend could reassert. The trimmed sizing was appropriate given stretch, but the holding window was too short to distinguish noise from a genuine reversal.
executed.risks_observed: Holding both XLE and XLK simultaneously creates a momentum-factor concentration at the basket level — both positions were selected on relative-strength signals, so a broad risk-off rotation would have hit both legs together with no hedge. The RSI-73 entry also left minimal buffer before a stop would be threatened on any intraday volatility spike.

uncommitted.rationale: Worst sector: gold -5.4% and silver -10.3% remove the commodity-inflation bid, dollar firming adds FX headwind, below both SMAs with negative MACD.
Oil as direct cost headwind for energy-intensive manufacturers; below both SMAs with MACD histogram at -0.638 and macro rated Mildly Bearish.
Squeezed by energy cost headwinds, below SMA20, MACD histogram deeply negative at -0.648, and macro rated Mildly Bearish.
Below both SMAs with deteriorating MACD; 30Y headwind is structural and macro view is explicitly Bearish — RSI at 35 limits fresh short entry but upside is capped.
Below SMA20 with negative MACD histogram; 30Y at 5.15% is a structural cap-rate headwind with no relief in sight.
MACD histogram turned negative as 5d momentum cracks; Nasdaq AI capex jitters and rising real rates are headwinds on the dominant 20d winner.
Mild 5d recovery but below SMA20, 20d negative, and MACD histogram slightly negative — no confirmed trend.
RSI neutral, MACD line below signal, and below SMA50 — no clear directional momentum despite mildly bullish macro stance.
Below SMA20 with negative MACD histogram; curve steepening tailwind is a medium-term story, not a today catalyst.
Below both MAs, MACD histogram negative, dollar strength offsets any commodity upside, and macro is Mildly Bearish.
RSI 34 is near oversold but trend is firmly down — MACD fully negative and expanding lower, structural rate headwind with no imminent catalyst for relief.
Below SMA20, MACD histogram rolling negative, and energy cost pressure offsets any reshoring tailwind in the near term.
30Y at 5.13% with no cuts imminent is a structural ceiling; below SMA20, negative MACD histogram.
5d momentum has rolled to -2.08% despite dominant 20d strength — rate-hike panic headline and dollar headwind on export earnings are pulling the recent leader lower.
5d momentum is real but 20d is negative and MACD histogram is barely negative (-0.030), signalling fading short-term leadership with no medium-term confirmation.
Squeezed by higher energy costs and no rate relief; below SMA20, negative MACD histogram, and macro is explicitly Mildly Bearish.
Above both MAs and 5d is mildly positive, but MACD histogram is slightly negative and long-end steepening benefit is offset by 20d weakness and no clear catalyst today.
Below both MAs, MACD histogram negative, dollar strength offsets any commodity upside, and macro is Mildly Bearish.
RSI 34 is near oversold but trend is firmly down — MACD fully negative and expanding lower, structural rate headwind with no imminent catalyst for relief.
Below SMA20, MACD histogram rolling negative, and energy cost pressure offsets any reshoring tailwind in the near term.
30Y at 5.13% with no cuts imminent is a structural ceiling; below SMA20, negative MACD histogram.
5d momentum has rolled to -2.08% despite dominant 20d strength — rate-hike panic headline and dollar headwind on export earnings are pulling the recent leader lower.
5d momentum is real but 20d is negative and MACD histogram is barely negative (-0.030), signalling fading short-term leadership with no medium-term confirmation.
Squeezed by higher energy costs and no rate relief; below SMA20, negative MACD histogram, and macro is explicitly Mildly Bearish.
Above both MAs and 5d is mildly positive, but MACD histogram is slightly negative and long-end steepening benefit is offset by 20d weakness and no clear catalyst today.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Trades sector ETFs based on relative momentum and macro
alignment. US: SPDR sector ETFs (XLF, XLE, XLK, ...). UK/EU: iShares
STOXX Europe 600 sector ETFs (EXH1.DE, EXV3.DE, ...). Same strategy
bias, different ETF universe per region.

executed.thesis: Energy is the macro regime's primary beneficiary — USO at 149, supply shock intact, no demand-destruction signal. EXV3.DE confirms with +3.76%/+5.46% 5d/20d momentum, RSI 60.8, and price above both SMA20 and SMA50, directly mirroring the XLE leadership at the top of the US sector rankings.
executed.outcome_notes: The energy thesis held: price advanced intraday and the position closed modestly green, consistent with continued supply-shock tailwinds and XLE's sector leadership. The scheduled exit captured only a fraction of the available move — no trailing mechanism was in place to let a confirming trend run.
executed.risks_observed: Energy was the sole green leg in the basket, masking the defensive side's underperformance; a correlated commodity or geopolitical reversal intraday could have flipped the whole book negative simultaneously. With no intraday stop on the position, a sharp energy reversal after entry would have had no circuit-breaker.

executed.thesis: Healthcare is the defensive quality pick in a risk-off, rising-real-rates environment — mildly bullish in macro view, EXH1.DE shows +2.79%/+4.38% momentum across both timeframes with clean structure above SMA20 and SMA50, providing portfolio ballast if the Tech crack deepens.
executed.outcome_notes: Healthcare failed to function as ballast — price drifted lower through the session and the scheduled exit realised a small loss, suggesting the risk-off defensive bid was either absent or not strong enough to offset mild selling pressure in the name. The clean MA structure at entry did not translate into sustained buying.
executed.risks_observed: Both defensive legs (EXH1.DE and EXH5.DE) lost ground on the same day, indicating the 'defensive rotation' thesis was correlation risk disguised as diversification — a single-day risk-off reversal or sector-wide ETF outflow would have hit both simultaneously. The absence of any stop meant the loss was capped only by the scheduled exit, not by price action.

executed.thesis: Consumer Staples (Food & Beverage) leads the candidate set on 5d returns (+3.90%) and is the defensive rotation trade aligned with the macro stagflationary backdrop; above both MAs with a positive MACD, it rounds out a concentrated Energy + Defensives portfolio that avoids every macro-bearish sector.
executed.outcome_notes: Despite leading the candidate set on recent momentum, Consumer Staples reversed intraday and the position closed underwater, suggesting the prior 5d strength was mean-reverting rather than a breakout continuation. The scheduled exit prevented further drawdown but offered no active management of the deteriorating tape.
executed.risks_observed: The basket ran three positions with only one net winner, and the two losers were in adjacent defensive sub-sectors that moved in lockstep — the portfolio's effective sector diversification was lower than the ticker count implied. Momentum-at-entry as the primary signal carries look-back bias risk; a single strong 5d window can reflect an already-exhausted move rather than an ongoing one.

executed.thesis: EXH1.DE (European Oil & Gas) is the clearest regime trade: best 5d (+1.9%) and 20d (+5.6%) relative strength in the candidate set, above both SMA20 and SMA50, with the macro thesis fully aligned — the oil supply shock makes energy the highest-conviction sector rotation this week. Slightly tighter stop given established trend, wider take profit to capture the oil momentum leg.
executed.outcome_notes: Price drifted in the right direction but the oil momentum leg never accelerated — a scheduled exit rather than a take-profit hit signals the supply-shock catalyst was either already priced or failed to reprice within the holding window. The above-SMA positioning held and the trade closed green, but the move was consistent with noise rather than a regime trend.
executed.risks_observed: Single-sector energy concentration meant any intraday reversal in oil sentiment (OPEC commentary, demand revision) would have flipped this to a loss with minimal warning. The asymmetry of a tighter stop against a wider take-profit was not rewarded here, and the scheduled exit left potential upside on the table had momentum arrived later in the session.

executed.thesis: EXV3.DE (European Technology) is flagging a breakout: volume 3.69x the 20-day average is the strongest flow signal in the set today, price is above both SMAs, RSI at 61 with positive MACD histogram confirms the setup; the AI capex cycle remains intact per the macro view, making this a technically-confirmed entry into the second-ranked macro sector.
executed.outcome_notes: The volume spike did not produce price follow-through — exit below entry on a scheduled close suggests the 3.69x volume bar was more likely absorption or distribution than genuine accumulation, invalidating the breakout read at entry. RSI and MACD confirmed momentum at the time but were lagging the actual flow dynamic.
executed.risks_observed: Elevated volume without same-session price confirmation is a known false-breakout pattern; the signal weighting should distinguish between demand-driven and supply-driven volume spikes. Running this alongside EXH1.DE created a two-leg basket where a broad risk-off move would have hit both simultaneously, and with neither stop triggered on the day, full notional was exposed throughout — no safety net fired.

executed.thesis: European Energy ETF aligned with the top-ranked macro theme — oil +27.7% 20d, sector +7.15% 20d, holding above both SMA20 and SMA50 with RSI 60 (momentum without being overbought); the mild 5d pullback (-0.10%) into MACD support is a clean entry within an intact uptrend.
executed.outcome_notes: Price was unchanged at exit, confirming neither the uptrend continuation nor any reversal — the position was simply held through the scheduled window without a price catalyst. The scheduled exit prevented any capture of the momentum the thesis anticipated, suggesting the holding period was too short relative to the macro driver's cycle. No information was generated about whether the MACD support level was meaningful.
executed.risks_observed: Energy ETFs carry sharp reversal risk if oil momentum stalls or geopolitical supply narrative shifts intraday — a flat exit masks the fact that no stop was tested, not that no risk existed. Single-commodity-linked sector concentration means correlation to oil swings is essentially unhedged at the basket level, and both positions here share European-listed exposure, compounding EUR FX drag risk.

executed.thesis: Defensive sector (likely Healthcare) fitting the stagflationary rotation backdrop where defensives are re-asserting; elevated volume ratio (1.90x avg) signals institutional interest and the MACD histogram (+0.309) is expanding with RSI at 52 — room to run vs the more extended Energy position.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit on schedule with no price movement; the elevated volume ratio that flagged institutional interest did not translate into observable price action during the holding window, so the signal remains unconfirmed. The MACD expansion thesis was not tested — the scheduled exit pre-empted any read on whether the setup was leading or lagging.
executed.risks_observed: A 1.90x volume spike is ambiguous without knowing the direction of that volume — if it was distribution rather than accumulation, the entry was into supply rather than demand. At the basket level, holding both a momentum-Energy and a defensive-Healthcare position simultaneously dilutes the rotation conviction: if stagflation is the thesis, sizing should reflect a cleaner directional view rather than hedging within the book.

uncommitted.rationale: Utilities-like ETF with 20d -6.36% — today's 5d bounce (+2.92%) is a dead-cat against structurally bearish backdrop of 30Y at 5.15%; elevated volume (1.13x) may be distribution.
uncommitted.reflection: Dead-cat bounce call was directionally right but the move reverted to flat rather than continuing down — distribution signal overstated near-term selling pressure.

uncommitted.rationale: Tech ETF below SMA20, 5d -2.88% mirrors the XLK crack flagged in macro view; MACD histogram negative and turning; rising real rates and firming dollar are structural headwinds.
uncommitted.reflection: MACD-negative + below-SMA20 + real-rate headwinds all fired; XLK crack analogy held and the ETF dropped harder than predicted.

uncommitted.rationale: Automobiles above both MAs but MACD losing momentum and 20d still negative — macro headwinds on consumer discretionary via energy costs and stagnant real wages.
uncommitted.reflection: MACD deceleration flagged correctly but above-MA position absorbed macro headwinds, keeping the move within flat-zone despite negative 20d drag.

uncommitted.rationale: MACD turning mildly bullish and above both MAs, but macro strongly bearish on Materials (gold -5.4%, silver -10.3%, real rates rising, dollar firming) caps upside.
uncommitted.reflection: Above-MA / mild-MACD-turn read as flat upside, but macro bearishness on Materials (gold/silver collapse, dollar strength) overwhelmed technicals and drove a sharp decline.

uncommitted.rationale: Broad European small-cap with mild 5d gain but no clear sector leadership signal; MACD histogram slightly negative, 20d barely negative — no compelling edge.
uncommitted.reflection: No-edge call confirmed — MACD and 20d signals were too weak to give direction, and the ETF stayed effectively flat.

uncommitted.rationale: Below both MAs, RSI 45.9, MACD negative and accelerating lower — telecom/media has no macro tailwind and the technical structure is the weakest in the set outside Materials.
uncommitted.reflection: Bearish technical structure (sub-50 RSI, accelerating MACD decline) was real but the move stopped at the flat/mild-down boundary rather than extending.

uncommitted.rationale: Industrials below SMA20, MACD histogram deeply negative (-0.375), 20d -3.05% — energy as direct cost headwind confirmed by macro view; rolling over from recent attempt.
uncommitted.reflection: Deeply negative MACD histogram and energy-cost headwind predicted mild-down correctly, though actual decline was roughly twice the magnitude forecast.

uncommitted.rationale: Banks below SMA50, 20d -4.12%, RSI sub-50 — curve steepening is a medium-term NIM tailwind but near-term momentum is absent and macro rates on hold indefinitely.
uncommitted.reflection: Rationale focused on absent near-term momentum and ignored curve-steepening NIM tailwind, which drove a sustained rally instead of the expected mild decline.

uncommitted.rationale: Below both SMAs, RSI 41 and falling, negative on both 5d and 20d with no macro catalyst for insurance rerating.
